src/backend/catalog/aclchk.c
1/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 *
3 * aclchk.c
4 *	  Routines to check access control permissions.
5 *
6 * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
7 * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
8 *
9 *
10 * IDENTIFICATION
11 *	  src/backend/catalog/aclchk.c
12 *
13 * NOTES
14 *	  See acl.h.
15 *
16 *	  The xxx_aclmask() functions in this file are wrappers around
17 *	  acl.c's aclmask() function; see that for basic usage information.
18 *	  The wrapper functions add object-type-specific lookup capability.
19 *	  Generally, they will throw error if the object doesn't exist.
20 *
21 *	  The xxx_aclmask_ext() functions add the ability to not throw
22 *	  error if the object doesn't exist.  If their "is_missing" argument
23 *	  isn't NULL, then when the object isn't found they will set
24 *	  *is_missing = true and return zero (no privileges) instead of
25 *	  throwing an error.  Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.
26 *
27 *	  The xxx_aclcheck() functions are simplified wrappers around the
28 *	  corresponding xxx_aclmask() functions, simply returning ACLCHECK_OK
29 *	  if any of the privileges specified in "mode" are held, and otherwise
30 *	  a suitable error code (in practice, always ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV).
31 *	  Again, they will throw error if the object doesn't exist.
32 *
33 *	  The xxx_aclcheck_ext() functions add the ability to not throw
34 *	  error if the object doesn't exist.  Their "is_missing" argument
35 *	  works similarly to the xxx_aclmask_ext() functions.
36 *
37 *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
38 */

88/*
89 * Internal format used by ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES.
90 */
91typedef struct
92{
93	Oid			roleid;			/* owning role */
94	Oid			nspid;			/* namespace, or InvalidOid if none */
95	/* remaining fields are same as in InternalGrant: */
96	bool		is_grant;
97	ObjectType	objtype;
98	bool		all_privs;
99	AclMode		privileges;
100	List	   *grantees;
101	bool		grant_option;
102	DropBehavior behavior;
103} InternalDefaultACL;
104
105/*
106 * When performing a binary-upgrade, pg_dump will call a function to set
107 * this variable to let us know that we need to populate the pg_init_privs
108 * table for the GRANT/REVOKE commands while this variable is set to true.
109 */
110bool		binary_upgrade_record_init_privs = false;
111
112static void ExecGrantStmt_oids(InternalGrant *istmt);
113static void ExecGrant_Relation(InternalGrant *istmt);
114static void ExecGrant_common(InternalGrant *istmt, Oid classid, AclMode default_privs,
115							 void (*object_check) (InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple));
116static void ExecGrant_Language_check(InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple);
117static void ExecGrant_Largeobject(InternalGrant *istmt);
118static void ExecGrant_Type_check(InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple);
119static void ExecGrant_Parameter(InternalGrant *istmt);
120
121static void SetDefaultACLsInSchemas(InternalDefaultACL *iacls, List *nspnames);
122static void SetDefaultACL(InternalDefaultACL *iacls);
123
124static List *objectNamesToOids(ObjectType objtype, List *objnames,
125							   bool is_grant);
126static List *objectsInSchemaToOids(ObjectType objtype, List *nspnames);
127static List *getRelationsInNamespace(Oid namespaceId, char relkind);
128static void expand_col_privileges(List *colnames, Oid table_oid,
129								  AclMode this_privileges,
130								  AclMode *col_privileges,
131								  int num_col_privileges);
132static void expand_all_col_privileges(Oid table_oid, Form_pg_class classForm,
133									  AclMode this_privileges,
134									  AclMode *col_privileges,
135									  int num_col_privileges);
136static AclMode string_to_privilege(const char *privname);
137static const char *privilege_to_string(AclMode privilege);
138static AclMode restrict_and_check_grant(bool is_grant, AclMode avail_goptions,
139										bool all_privs, AclMode privileges,
140										Oid objectId, Oid grantorId,
141										ObjectType objtype, const char *objname,
142										AttrNumber att_number, const char *colname);
143static AclMode pg_aclmask(ObjectType objtype, Oid object_oid, AttrNumber attnum,
144						  Oid roleid, AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how);
145static AclMode object_aclmask(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid,
146							  AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how);
147static AclMode object_aclmask_ext(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid,
148								  AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
149								  bool *is_missing);
150static AclMode pg_attribute_aclmask(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum,
151									Oid roleid, AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how);
152static AclMode pg_attribute_aclmask_ext(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum,
153										Oid roleid, AclMode mask,
154										AclMaskHow how, bool *is_missing);
155static AclMode pg_class_aclmask_ext(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid,
156									AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
157									bool *is_missing);
158static AclMode pg_parameter_acl_aclmask(Oid acl_oid, Oid roleid,
159										AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how);
160static AclMode pg_largeobject_aclmask_snapshot(Oid lobj_oid, Oid roleid,
161											   AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how, Snapshot snapshot);
162static AclMode pg_namespace_aclmask_ext(Oid nsp_oid, Oid roleid,
163										AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
164										bool *is_missing);
165static AclMode pg_type_aclmask_ext(Oid type_oid, Oid roleid,
166								   AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
167								   bool *is_missing);
168static void recordExtensionInitPriv(Oid objoid, Oid classoid, int objsubid,
169									Acl *new_acl);
170static void recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(Oid objoid, Oid classoid, int objsubid,
171										  Acl *new_acl);
172
173
174/*
175 * If is_grant is true, adds the given privileges for the list of
176 * grantees to the existing old_acl.  If is_grant is false, the
177 * privileges for the given grantees are removed from old_acl.
178 *
179 * NB: the original old_acl is pfree'd.
180 */
181static Acl *
182merge_acl_with_grant(Acl *old_acl, bool is_grant,
183					 bool grant_option, DropBehavior behavior,
184					 List *grantees, AclMode privileges,
185					 Oid grantorId, Oid ownerId)
186{
187	unsigned	modechg;
188	ListCell   *j;
189	Acl		   *new_acl;
190
191	modechg = is_grant ? ACL_MODECHG_ADD : ACL_MODECHG_DEL;
192
193	new_acl = old_acl;
194
195	foreach(j, grantees)
196	{
197		AclItem		aclitem;
198		Acl		   *newer_acl;
199
200		aclitem.ai_grantee = lfirst_oid(j);
201
202		/*
203		 * Grant options can only be granted to individual roles, not PUBLIC.
204		 * The reason is that if a user would re-grant a privilege that he
205		 * held through PUBLIC, and later the user is removed, the situation
206		 * is impossible to clean up.
207		 */
208		if (is_grant && grant_option && aclitem.ai_grantee == ACL_ID_PUBLIC)
209			ereport(ERROR,
210					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
211					 errmsg("grant options can only be granted to roles")));
212
213		aclitem.ai_grantor = grantorId;
214
215		/*
216		 * The asymmetry in the conditions here comes from the spec.  In
217		 * GRANT, the grant_option flag signals WITH GRANT OPTION, which means
218		 * to grant both the basic privilege and its grant option. But in
219		 * REVOKE, plain revoke revokes both the basic privilege and its grant
220		 * option, while REVOKE GRANT OPTION revokes only the option.
221		 */
222		ACLITEM_SET_PRIVS_GOPTIONS(aclitem,
223								   (is_grant || !grant_option) ? privileges : ACL_NO_RIGHTS,
224								   (!is_grant || grant_option) ? privileges : ACL_NO_RIGHTS);
225
226		newer_acl = aclupdate(new_acl, &aclitem, modechg, ownerId, behavior);
227
228		/* avoid memory leak when there are many grantees */
229		pfree(new_acl);
230		new_acl = newer_acl;
231	}
232
233	return new_acl;
234}
235
236/*
237 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit
238 * the standards-mandated warning and error messages.
239 */
240static AclMode
241restrict_and_check_grant(bool is_grant, AclMode avail_goptions, bool all_privs,
242						 AclMode privileges, Oid objectId, Oid grantorId,
243						 ObjectType objtype, const char *objname,
244						 AttrNumber att_number, const char *colname)
245{
246	AclMode		this_privileges;
247	AclMode		whole_mask;
248
249	switch (objtype)
250	{
251		case OBJECT_COLUMN:
252			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN;
253			break;
254		case OBJECT_TABLE:
255			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION;
256			break;
257		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
258			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
259			break;
260		case OBJECT_DATABASE:
261			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_DATABASE;
262			break;
263		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
264			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
265			break;
266		case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
267			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LANGUAGE;
268			break;
269		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
270			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LARGEOBJECT;
271			break;
272		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
273			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA;
274			break;
275		case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
276			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TABLESPACE;
277			break;
278		case OBJECT_FDW:
279			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FDW;
280			break;
281		case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
282			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FOREIGN_SERVER;
283			break;
284		case OBJECT_EVENT_TRIGGER:
285			elog(ERROR, "grantable rights not supported for event triggers");
286			/* not reached, but keep compiler quiet */
287			return ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
288		case OBJECT_TYPE:
289			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE;
290			break;
291		case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
292			whole_mask = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_PARAMETER_ACL;
293			break;
294		default:
295			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized object type: %d", objtype);
296			/* not reached, but keep compiler quiet */
297			return ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
298	}
299
300	/*
301	 * If we found no grant options, consider whether to issue a hard error.
302	 * Per spec, having any privilege at all on the object will get you by
303	 * here.
304	 */
305	if (avail_goptions == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
306	{
307		if (pg_aclmask(objtype, objectId, att_number, grantorId,
308					   whole_mask | ACL_GRANT_OPTION_FOR(whole_mask),
309					   ACLMASK_ANY) == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
310		{
311			if (objtype == OBJECT_COLUMN && colname)
312				aclcheck_error_col(ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV, objtype, objname, colname);
313			else
314				aclcheck_error(ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV, objtype, objname);
315		}
316	}
317
318	/*
319	 * Restrict the operation to what we can actually grant or revoke, and
320	 * issue a warning if appropriate.  (For REVOKE this isn't quite what the
321	 * spec says to do: the spec seems to want a warning only if no privilege
322	 * bits actually change in the ACL. In practice that behavior seems much
323	 * too noisy, as well as inconsistent with the GRANT case.)
324	 */
325	this_privileges = privileges & ACL_OPTION_TO_PRIVS(avail_goptions);
326	if (is_grant)
327	{
328		if (this_privileges == 0)
329		{
330			if (objtype == OBJECT_COLUMN && colname)
331				ereport(WARNING,
332						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_GRANTED),
333						 errmsg("no privileges were granted for column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\"",
334								colname, objname)));
335			else
336				ereport(WARNING,
337						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_GRANTED),
338						 errmsg("no privileges were granted for \"%s\"",
339								objname)));
340		}
341		else if (!all_privs && this_privileges != privileges)
342		{
343			if (objtype == OBJECT_COLUMN && colname)
344				ereport(WARNING,
345						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_GRANTED),
346						 errmsg("not all privileges were granted for column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\"",
347								colname, objname)));
348			else
349				ereport(WARNING,
350						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_GRANTED),
351						 errmsg("not all privileges were granted for \"%s\"",
352								objname)));
353		}
354	}
355	else
356	{
357		if (this_privileges == 0)
358		{
359			if (objtype == OBJECT_COLUMN && colname)
360				ereport(WARNING,
361						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_REVOKED),
362						 errmsg("no privileges could be revoked for column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\"",
363								colname, objname)));
364			else
365				ereport(WARNING,
366						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_REVOKED),
367						 errmsg("no privileges could be revoked for \"%s\"",
368								objname)));
369		}
370		else if (!all_privs && this_privileges != privileges)
371		{
372			if (objtype == OBJECT_COLUMN && colname)
373				ereport(WARNING,
374						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_REVOKED),
375						 errmsg("not all privileges could be revoked for column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\"",
376								colname, objname)));
377			else
378				ereport(WARNING,
379						(errcode(ERRCODE_WARNING_PRIVILEGE_NOT_REVOKED),
380						 errmsg("not all privileges could be revoked for \"%s\"",
381								objname)));
382		}
383	}
384
385	return this_privileges;
386}
387
388/*
389 * Called to execute the utility commands GRANT and REVOKE
390 */
391void
392ExecuteGrantStmt(GrantStmt *stmt)
393{
394	InternalGrant istmt;
395	ListCell   *cell;
396	const char *errormsg;
397	AclMode		all_privileges;
398
399	if (stmt->grantor)
400	{
401		Oid			grantor;
402
403		grantor = get_rolespec_oid(stmt->grantor, false);
404
405		/*
406		 * Currently, this clause is only for SQL compatibility, not very
407		 * interesting otherwise.
408		 */
409		if (grantor != GetUserId())
410			ereport(ERROR,
411					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
412					 errmsg("grantor must be current user")));
413	}
414
415	/*
416	 * Turn the regular GrantStmt into the InternalGrant form.
417	 */
418	istmt.is_grant = stmt->is_grant;
419	istmt.objtype = stmt->objtype;
420
421	/* Collect the OIDs of the target objects */
422	switch (stmt->targtype)
423	{
424		case ACL_TARGET_OBJECT:
425			istmt.objects = objectNamesToOids(stmt->objtype, stmt->objects,
426											  stmt->is_grant);
427			break;
428		case ACL_TARGET_ALL_IN_SCHEMA:
429			istmt.objects = objectsInSchemaToOids(stmt->objtype, stmt->objects);
430			break;
431			/* ACL_TARGET_DEFAULTS should not be seen here */
432		default:
433			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized GrantStmt.targtype: %d",
434				 (int) stmt->targtype);
435	}
436
437	/* all_privs to be filled below */
438	/* privileges to be filled below */
439	istmt.col_privs = NIL;		/* may get filled below */
440	istmt.grantees = NIL;		/* filled below */
441	istmt.grant_option = stmt->grant_option;
442	istmt.behavior = stmt->behavior;
443
444	/*
445	 * Convert the RoleSpec list into an Oid list.  Note that at this point we
446	 * insert an ACL_ID_PUBLIC into the list if appropriate, so downstream
447	 * there shouldn't be any additional work needed to support this case.
448	 */
449	foreach(cell, stmt->grantees)
450	{
451		RoleSpec   *grantee = (RoleSpec *) lfirst(cell);
452		Oid			grantee_uid;
453
454		switch (grantee->roletype)
455		{
456			case ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:
457				grantee_uid = ACL_ID_PUBLIC;
458				break;
459			default:
460				grantee_uid = get_rolespec_oid(grantee, false);
461				break;
462		}
463		istmt.grantees = lappend_oid(istmt.grantees, grantee_uid);
464	}
465
466	/*
467	 * Convert stmt->privileges, a list of AccessPriv nodes, into an AclMode
468	 * bitmask.  Note: objtype can't be OBJECT_COLUMN.
469	 */
470	switch (stmt->objtype)
471	{
472		case OBJECT_TABLE:
473
474			/*
475			 * Because this might be a sequence, we test both relation and
476			 * sequence bits, and later do a more limited test when we know
477			 * the object type.
478			 */
479			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION | ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
480			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for relation");
481			break;
482		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
483			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
484			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for sequence");
485			break;
486		case OBJECT_DATABASE:
487			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_DATABASE;
488			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for database");
489			break;
490		case OBJECT_DOMAIN:
491			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE;
492			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for domain");
493			break;
494		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
495			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
496			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for function");
497			break;
498		case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
499			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LANGUAGE;
500			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for language");
501			break;
502		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
503			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LARGEOBJECT;
504			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for large object");
505			break;
506		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
507			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA;
508			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for schema");
509			break;
510		case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
511			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
512			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for procedure");
513			break;
514		case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
515			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
516			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for routine");
517			break;
518		case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
519			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TABLESPACE;
520			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for tablespace");
521			break;
522		case OBJECT_TYPE:
523			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE;
524			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for type");
525			break;
526		case OBJECT_FDW:
527			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FDW;
528			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for foreign-data wrapper");
529			break;
530		case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
531			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FOREIGN_SERVER;
532			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for foreign server");
533			break;
534		case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
535			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_PARAMETER_ACL;
536			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for parameter");
537			break;
538		default:
539			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized GrantStmt.objtype: %d",
540				 (int) stmt->objtype);
541			/* keep compiler quiet */
542			all_privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
543			errormsg = NULL;
544	}
545
546	if (stmt->privileges == NIL)
547	{
548		istmt.all_privs = true;
549
550		/*
551		 * will be turned into ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_* by the internal routines
552		 * depending on the object type
553		 */
554		istmt.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
555	}
556	else
557	{
558		istmt.all_privs = false;
559		istmt.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
560
561		foreach(cell, stmt->privileges)
562		{
563			AccessPriv *privnode = (AccessPriv *) lfirst(cell);
564			AclMode		priv;
565
566			/*
567			 * If it's a column-level specification, we just set it aside in
568			 * col_privs for the moment; but insist it's for a relation.
569			 */
570			if (privnode->cols)
571			{
572				if (stmt->objtype != OBJECT_TABLE)
573					ereport(ERROR,
574							(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
575							 errmsg("column privileges are only valid for relations")));
576				istmt.col_privs = lappend(istmt.col_privs, privnode);
577				continue;
578			}
579
580			if (privnode->priv_name == NULL)	/* parser mistake? */
581				elog(ERROR, "AccessPriv node must specify privilege or columns");
582			priv = string_to_privilege(privnode->priv_name);
583
584			if (priv & ~((AclMode) all_privileges))
585				ereport(ERROR,
586						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
587						 errmsg(errormsg, privilege_to_string(priv))));
588
589			istmt.privileges |= priv;
590		}
591	}
592
593	ExecGrantStmt_oids(&istmt);
594}
595
596/*
597 * ExecGrantStmt_oids
598 *
599 * Internal entry point for granting and revoking privileges.
600 */
601static void
602ExecGrantStmt_oids(InternalGrant *istmt)
603{
604	switch (istmt->objtype)
605	{
606		case OBJECT_TABLE:
607		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
608			ExecGrant_Relation(istmt);
609			break;
610		case OBJECT_DATABASE:
611			ExecGrant_common(istmt, DatabaseRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_DATABASE, NULL);
612			break;
613		case OBJECT_DOMAIN:
614		case OBJECT_TYPE:
615			ExecGrant_common(istmt, TypeRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE, ExecGrant_Type_check);
616			break;
617		case OBJECT_FDW:
618			ExecGrant_common(istmt, ForeignDataWrapperRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FDW, NULL);
619			break;
620		case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
621			ExecGrant_common(istmt, ForeignServerRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FOREIGN_SERVER, NULL);
622			break;
623		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
624		case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
625		case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
626			ExecGrant_common(istmt, ProcedureRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION, NULL);
627			break;
628		case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
629			ExecGrant_common(istmt, LanguageRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LANGUAGE, ExecGrant_Language_check);
630			break;
631		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
632			ExecGrant_Largeobject(istmt);
633			break;
634		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
635			ExecGrant_common(istmt, NamespaceRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA, NULL);
636			break;
637		case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
638			ExecGrant_common(istmt, TableSpaceRelationId, ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TABLESPACE, NULL);
639			break;
640		case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
641			ExecGrant_Parameter(istmt);
642			break;
643		default:
644			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized GrantStmt.objtype: %d",
645				 (int) istmt->objtype);
646	}
647
648	/*
649	 * Pass the info to event triggers about the just-executed GRANT.  Note
650	 * that we prefer to do it after actually executing it, because that gives
651	 * the functions a chance to adjust the istmt with privileges actually
652	 * granted.
653	 */
654	if (EventTriggerSupportsObjectType(istmt->objtype))
655		EventTriggerCollectGrant(istmt);
656}
657
658/*
659 * objectNamesToOids
660 *
661 * Turn a list of object names of a given type into an Oid list.
662 *
663 * XXX This function intentionally takes only an AccessShareLock.  In the face
664 * of concurrent DDL, we might easily latch onto an old version of an object,
665 * causing the GRANT or REVOKE statement to fail.  But it does prevent the
666 * object from disappearing altogether.  To do better, we would need to use a
667 * self-exclusive lock, perhaps ShareUpdateExclusiveLock, here and before
668 * *every* CatalogTupleUpdate() of a row that GRANT/REVOKE can affect.
669 * Besides that additional work, this could have operational costs.  For
670 * example, it would make GRANT ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA terminate every
671 * autovacuum running in the schema and consume a shared lock table entry per
672 * table in the schema.  The user-visible benefit of that additional work is
673 * just changing "ERROR: tuple concurrently updated" to blocking.  That's not
674 * nothing, but it might not outweigh autovacuum termination and lock table
675 * consumption spikes.
676 */
677static List *
678objectNamesToOids(ObjectType objtype, List *objnames, bool is_grant)
679{
680	List	   *objects = NIL;
681	ListCell   *cell;
682	const LOCKMODE lockmode = AccessShareLock;
683
684	Assert(objnames != NIL);
685
686	switch (objtype)
687	{
688		default:
689
690			/*
691			 * For most object types, we use get_object_address() directly.
692			 */
693			foreach(cell, objnames)
694			{
695				ObjectAddress address;
696
697				address = get_object_address(objtype, lfirst(cell), NULL, lockmode, false);
698				objects = lappend_oid(objects, address.objectId);
699			}
700			break;
701
702		case OBJECT_TABLE:
703		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
704
705			/*
706			 * Here, we don't use get_object_address().  It requires that the
707			 * specified object type match the actual type of the object, but
708			 * in GRANT/REVOKE, all table-like things are addressed as TABLE.
709			 */
710			foreach(cell, objnames)
711			{
712				RangeVar   *relvar = (RangeVar *) lfirst(cell);
713				Oid			relOid;
714
715				relOid = RangeVarGetRelid(relvar, lockmode, false);
716				objects = lappend_oid(objects, relOid);
717			}
718			break;
719
720		case OBJECT_DOMAIN:
721		case OBJECT_TYPE:
722
723			/*
724			 * The parse representation of types and domains in privilege
725			 * targets is different from that expected by get_object_address()
726			 * (for parse conflict reasons), so we have to do a bit of
727			 * conversion here.
728			 */
729			foreach(cell, objnames)
730			{
731				List	   *typname = (List *) lfirst(cell);
732				TypeName   *tn = makeTypeNameFromNameList(typname);
733				ObjectAddress address;
734				Relation	relation;
735
736				address = get_object_address(objtype, (Node *) tn, &relation, lockmode, false);
737				Assert(relation == NULL);
738				objects = lappend_oid(objects, address.objectId);
739			}
740			break;
741
742		case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
743
744			/*
745			 * Parameters are handled completely differently.
746			 */
747			foreach(cell, objnames)
748			{
749				/*
750				 * In this code we represent a GUC by the OID of its entry in
751				 * pg_parameter_acl, which we have to manufacture here if it
752				 * doesn't exist yet.  (That's a hack for sure, but it avoids
753				 * messing with all the GRANT/REVOKE infrastructure that
754				 * expects to use OIDs for object identities.)  However, if
755				 * this is a REVOKE, we can instead just ignore any GUCs that
756				 * don't have such an entry, as they must not have any
757				 * privileges needing removal.
758				 */
759				char	   *parameter = strVal(lfirst(cell));
760				Oid			parameterId = ParameterAclLookup(parameter, true);
761
762				if (!OidIsValid(parameterId) && is_grant)
763				{
764					parameterId = ParameterAclCreate(parameter);
765
766					/*
767					 * Prevent error when processing duplicate objects, and
768					 * make this new entry visible so that ExecGrant_Parameter
769					 * can update it.
770					 */
771					CommandCounterIncrement();
772				}
773				if (OidIsValid(parameterId))
774					objects = lappend_oid(objects, parameterId);
775			}
776			break;
777	}
778
779	return objects;
780}
781
782/*
783 * objectsInSchemaToOids
784 *
785 * Find all objects of a given type in specified schemas, and make a list
786 * of their Oids.  We check USAGE privilege on the schemas, but there is
787 * no privilege checking on the individual objects here.
788 */
789static List *
790objectsInSchemaToOids(ObjectType objtype, List *nspnames)
791{
792	List	   *objects = NIL;
793	ListCell   *cell;
794
795	foreach(cell, nspnames)
796	{
797		char	   *nspname = strVal(lfirst(cell));
798		Oid			namespaceId;
799		List	   *objs;
800
801		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(nspname, false);
802
803		switch (objtype)
804		{
805			case OBJECT_TABLE:
806				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_RELATION);
807				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
808				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_VIEW);
809				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
810				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_MATVIEW);
811				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
812				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE);
813				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
814				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
815				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
816				break;
817			case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
818				objs = getRelationsInNamespace(namespaceId, RELKIND_SEQUENCE);
819				objects = list_concat(objects, objs);
820				break;
821			case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
822			case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
823			case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
824				{
825					ScanKeyData key[2];
826					int			keycount;
827					Relation	rel;
828					TableScanDesc scan;
829					HeapTuple	tuple;
830
831					keycount = 0;
832					ScanKeyInit(&key[keycount++],
833								Anum_pg_proc_pronamespace,
834								BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
835								ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
836
837					if (objtype == OBJECT_FUNCTION)
838						/* includes aggregates and window functions */
839						ScanKeyInit(&key[keycount++],
840									Anum_pg_proc_prokind,
841									BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_CHARNE,
842									CharGetDatum(PROKIND_PROCEDURE));
843					else if (objtype == OBJECT_PROCEDURE)
844						ScanKeyInit(&key[keycount++],
845									Anum_pg_proc_prokind,
846									BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_CHAREQ,
847									CharGetDatum(PROKIND_PROCEDURE));
848
849					rel = table_open(ProcedureRelationId, AccessShareLock);
850					scan = table_beginscan_catalog(rel, keycount, key);
851
852					while ((tuple = heap_getnext(scan, ForwardScanDirection)) != NULL)
853					{
854						Oid			oid = ((Form_pg_proc) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->oid;
855
856						objects = lappend_oid(objects, oid);
857					}
858
859					table_endscan(scan);
860					table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
861				}
862				break;
863			default:
864				/* should not happen */
865				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized GrantStmt.objtype: %d",
866					 (int) objtype);
867		}
868	}
869
870	return objects;
871}
872
873/*
874 * getRelationsInNamespace
875 *
876 * Return Oid list of relations in given namespace filtered by relation kind
877 */
878static List *
879getRelationsInNamespace(Oid namespaceId, char relkind)
880{
881	List	   *relations = NIL;
882	ScanKeyData key[2];
883	Relation	rel;
884	TableScanDesc scan;
885	HeapTuple	tuple;
886
887	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
888				Anum_pg_class_relnamespace,
889				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
890				ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
891	ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
892				Anum_pg_class_relkind,
893				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_CHAREQ,
894				CharGetDatum(relkind));
895
896	rel = table_open(RelationRelationId, AccessShareLock);
897	scan = table_beginscan_catalog(rel, 2, key);
898
899	while ((tuple = heap_getnext(scan, ForwardScanDirection)) != NULL)
900	{
901		Oid			oid = ((Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->oid;
902
903		relations = lappend_oid(relations, oid);
904	}
905
906	table_endscan(scan);
907	table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
908
909	return relations;
910}
911
912
913/*
914 * ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES statement
915 */
916void
917ExecAlterDefaultPrivilegesStmt(ParseState *pstate, AlterDefaultPrivilegesStmt *stmt)
918{
919	GrantStmt  *action = stmt->action;
920	InternalDefaultACL iacls;
921	ListCell   *cell;
922	List	   *rolespecs = NIL;
923	List	   *nspnames = NIL;
924	DefElem    *drolespecs = NULL;
925	DefElem    *dnspnames = NULL;
926	AclMode		all_privileges;
927	const char *errormsg;
928
929	/* Deconstruct the "options" part of the statement */
930	foreach(cell, stmt->options)
931	{
932		DefElem    *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(cell);
933
934		if (strcmp(defel->defname, "schemas") == 0)
935		{
936			if (dnspnames)
937				errorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);
938			dnspnames = defel;
939		}
940		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "roles") == 0)
941		{
942			if (drolespecs)
943				errorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);
944			drolespecs = defel;
945		}
946		else
947			elog(ERROR, "option \"%s\" not recognized", defel->defname);
948	}
949
950	if (dnspnames)
951		nspnames = (List *) dnspnames->arg;
952	if (drolespecs)
953		rolespecs = (List *) drolespecs->arg;
954
955	/* Prepare the InternalDefaultACL representation of the statement */
956	/* roleid to be filled below */
957	/* nspid to be filled in SetDefaultACLsInSchemas */
958	iacls.is_grant = action->is_grant;
959	iacls.objtype = action->objtype;
960	/* all_privs to be filled below */
961	/* privileges to be filled below */
962	iacls.grantees = NIL;		/* filled below */
963	iacls.grant_option = action->grant_option;
964	iacls.behavior = action->behavior;
965
966	/*
967	 * Convert the RoleSpec list into an Oid list.  Note that at this point we
968	 * insert an ACL_ID_PUBLIC into the list if appropriate, so downstream
969	 * there shouldn't be any additional work needed to support this case.
970	 */
971	foreach(cell, action->grantees)
972	{
973		RoleSpec   *grantee = (RoleSpec *) lfirst(cell);
974		Oid			grantee_uid;
975
976		switch (grantee->roletype)
977		{
978			case ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:
979				grantee_uid = ACL_ID_PUBLIC;
980				break;
981			default:
982				grantee_uid = get_rolespec_oid(grantee, false);
983				break;
984		}
985		iacls.grantees = lappend_oid(iacls.grantees, grantee_uid);
986	}
987
988	/*
989	 * Convert action->privileges, a list of privilege strings, into an
990	 * AclMode bitmask.
991	 */
992	switch (action->objtype)
993	{
994		case OBJECT_TABLE:
995			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION;
996			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for relation");
997			break;
998		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
999			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
1000			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for sequence");
1001			break;
1002		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
1003			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
1004			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for function");
1005			break;
1006		case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
1007			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
1008			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for procedure");
1009			break;
1010		case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
1011			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
1012			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for routine");
1013			break;
1014		case OBJECT_TYPE:
1015			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE;
1016			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for type");
1017			break;
1018		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
1019			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA;
1020			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for schema");
1021			break;
1022		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
1023			all_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LARGEOBJECT;
1024			errormsg = gettext_noop("invalid privilege type %s for large object");
1025			break;
1026		default:
1027			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized GrantStmt.objtype: %d",
1028				 (int) action->objtype);
1029			/* keep compiler quiet */
1030			all_privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
1031			errormsg = NULL;
1032	}
1033
1034	if (action->privileges == NIL)
1035	{
1036		iacls.all_privs = true;
1037
1038		/*
1039		 * will be turned into ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_* by the internal routines
1040		 * depending on the object type
1041		 */
1042		iacls.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
1043	}
1044	else
1045	{
1046		iacls.all_privs = false;
1047		iacls.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
1048
1049		foreach(cell, action->privileges)
1050		{
1051			AccessPriv *privnode = (AccessPriv *) lfirst(cell);
1052			AclMode		priv;
1053
1054			if (privnode->cols)
1055				ereport(ERROR,
1056						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1057						 errmsg("default privileges cannot be set for columns")));
1058
1059			if (privnode->priv_name == NULL)	/* parser mistake? */
1060				elog(ERROR, "AccessPriv node must specify privilege");
1061			priv = string_to_privilege(privnode->priv_name);
1062
1063			if (priv & ~((AclMode) all_privileges))
1064				ereport(ERROR,
1065						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1066						 errmsg(errormsg, privilege_to_string(priv))));
1067
1068			iacls.privileges |= priv;
1069		}
1070	}
1071
1072	if (rolespecs == NIL)
1073	{
1074		/* Set permissions for myself */
1075		iacls.roleid = GetUserId();
1076
1077		SetDefaultACLsInSchemas(&iacls, nspnames);
1078	}
1079	else
1080	{
1081		/* Look up the role OIDs and do permissions checks */
1082		ListCell   *rolecell;
1083
1084		foreach(rolecell, rolespecs)
1085		{
1086			RoleSpec   *rolespec = lfirst(rolecell);
1087
1088			iacls.roleid = get_rolespec_oid(rolespec, false);
1089
1090			if (!has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), iacls.roleid))
1091				ereport(ERROR,
1092						(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
1093						 errmsg("permission denied to change default privileges")));
1094
1095			SetDefaultACLsInSchemas(&iacls, nspnames);
1096		}
1097	}
1098}
1099
1100/*
1101 * Process ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES for a list of target schemas
1102 *
1103 * All fields of *iacls except nspid were filled already
1104 */
1105static void
1106SetDefaultACLsInSchemas(InternalDefaultACL *iacls, List *nspnames)
1107{
1108	if (nspnames == NIL)
1109	{
1110		/* Set database-wide permissions if no schema was specified */
1111		iacls->nspid = InvalidOid;
1112
1113		SetDefaultACL(iacls);
1114	}
1115	else
1116	{
1117		/* Look up the schema OIDs and set permissions for each one */
1118		ListCell   *nspcell;
1119
1120		foreach(nspcell, nspnames)
1121		{
1122			char	   *nspname = strVal(lfirst(nspcell));
1123
1124			iacls->nspid = get_namespace_oid(nspname, false);
1125
1126			/*
1127			 * We used to insist that the target role have CREATE privileges
1128			 * on the schema, since without that it wouldn't be able to create
1129			 * an object for which these default privileges would apply.
1130			 * However, this check proved to be more confusing than helpful,
1131			 * and it also caused certain database states to not be
1132			 * dumpable/restorable, since revoking CREATE doesn't cause
1133			 * default privileges for the schema to go away.  So now, we just
1134			 * allow the ALTER; if the user lacks CREATE he'll find out when
1135			 * he tries to create an object.
1136			 */
1137
1138			SetDefaultACL(iacls);
1139		}
1140	}
1141}
1142
1143
1144/*
1145 * Create or update a pg_default_acl entry
1146 */
1147static void
1148SetDefaultACL(InternalDefaultACL *iacls)
1149{
1150	AclMode		this_privileges = iacls->privileges;
1151	char		objtype;
1152	Relation	rel;
1153	HeapTuple	tuple;
1154	bool		isNew;
1155	Acl		   *def_acl;
1156	Acl		   *old_acl;
1157	Acl		   *new_acl;
1158	HeapTuple	newtuple;
1159	int			noldmembers;
1160	int			nnewmembers;
1161	Oid		   *oldmembers;
1162	Oid		   *newmembers;
1163
1164	rel = table_open(DefaultAclRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
1165
1166	/*
1167	 * The default for a global entry is the hard-wired default ACL for the
1168	 * particular object type.  The default for non-global entries is an empty
1169	 * ACL.  This must be so because global entries replace the hard-wired
1170	 * defaults, while others are added on.
1171	 */
1172	if (!OidIsValid(iacls->nspid))
1173		def_acl = acldefault(iacls->objtype, iacls->roleid);
1174	else
1175		def_acl = make_empty_acl();
1176
1177	/*
1178	 * Convert ACL object type to pg_default_acl object type and handle
1179	 * all_privs option
1180	 */
1181	switch (iacls->objtype)
1182	{
1183		case OBJECT_TABLE:
1184			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_RELATION;
1185			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1186				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION;
1187			break;
1188
1189		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
1190			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_SEQUENCE;
1191			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1192				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
1193			break;
1194
1195		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
1196			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_FUNCTION;
1197			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1198				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_FUNCTION;
1199			break;
1200
1201		case OBJECT_TYPE:
1202			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_TYPE;
1203			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1204				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_TYPE;
1205			break;
1206
1207		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
1208			if (OidIsValid(iacls->nspid))
1209				ereport(ERROR,
1210						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1211						 errmsg("cannot use IN SCHEMA clause when using %s",
1212								"GRANT/REVOKE ON SCHEMAS")));
1213			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_NAMESPACE;
1214			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1215				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA;
1216			break;
1217
1218		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
1219			if (OidIsValid(iacls->nspid))
1220				ereport(ERROR,
1221						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1222						 errmsg("cannot use IN SCHEMA clause when using %s",
1223								"GRANT/REVOKE ON LARGE OBJECTS")));
1224			objtype = DEFACLOBJ_LARGEOBJECT;
1225			if (iacls->all_privs && this_privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1226				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LARGEOBJECT;
1227			break;
1228
1229		default:
1230			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized object type: %d",
1231				 (int) iacls->objtype);
1232			objtype = 0;		/* keep compiler quiet */
1233			break;
1234	}
1235
1236	/* Search for existing row for this object type in catalog */
1237	tuple = SearchSysCache3(DEFACLROLENSPOBJ,
1238							ObjectIdGetDatum(iacls->roleid),
1239							ObjectIdGetDatum(iacls->nspid),
1240							CharGetDatum(objtype));
1241
1242	if (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
1243	{
1244		Datum		aclDatum;
1245		bool		isNull;
1246
1247		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(DEFACLROLENSPOBJ, tuple,
1248								   Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclacl,
1249								   &isNull);
1250		if (!isNull)
1251			old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
1252		else
1253			old_acl = NULL;		/* this case shouldn't happen, probably */
1254		isNew = false;
1255	}
1256	else
1257	{
1258		old_acl = NULL;
1259		isNew = true;
1260	}
1261
1262	if (old_acl != NULL)
1263	{
1264		/*
1265		 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
1266		 * shared dependency information.  Collect data before
1267		 * merge_acl_with_grant throws away old_acl.
1268		 */
1269		noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
1270	}
1271	else
1272	{
1273		/* If no or null entry, start with the default ACL value */
1274		old_acl = aclcopy(def_acl);
1275		/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
1276		noldmembers = 0;
1277		oldmembers = NULL;
1278	}
1279
1280	/*
1281	 * Generate new ACL.  Grantor of rights is always the same as the target
1282	 * role.
1283	 */
1284	new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl,
1285								   iacls->is_grant,
1286								   iacls->grant_option,
1287								   iacls->behavior,
1288								   iacls->grantees,
1289								   this_privileges,
1290								   iacls->roleid,
1291								   iacls->roleid);
1292
1293	/*
1294	 * If the result is the same as the default value, we do not need an
1295	 * explicit pg_default_acl entry, and should in fact remove the entry if
1296	 * it exists.  Must sort both arrays to compare properly.
1297	 */
1298	aclitemsort(new_acl);
1299	aclitemsort(def_acl);
1300	if (aclequal(new_acl, def_acl))
1301	{
1302		/* delete old entry, if indeed there is one */
1303		if (!isNew)
1304		{
1305			ObjectAddress myself;
1306
1307			/*
1308			 * The dependency machinery will take care of removing all
1309			 * associated dependency entries.  We use DROP_RESTRICT since
1310			 * there shouldn't be anything depending on this entry.
1311			 */
1312			myself.classId = DefaultAclRelationId;
1313			myself.objectId = ((Form_pg_default_acl) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->oid;
1314			myself.objectSubId = 0;
1315
1316			performDeletion(&myself, DROP_RESTRICT, 0);
1317		}
1318	}
1319	else
1320	{
1321		Datum		values[Natts_pg_default_acl] = {0};
1322		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_default_acl] = {0};
1323		bool		replaces[Natts_pg_default_acl] = {0};
1324		Oid			defAclOid;
1325
1326		if (isNew)
1327		{
1328			/* insert new entry */
1329			defAclOid = GetNewOidWithIndex(rel, DefaultAclOidIndexId,
1330										   Anum_pg_default_acl_oid);
1331			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_oid - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(defAclOid);
1332			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclrole - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(iacls->roleid);
1333			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclnamespace - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(iacls->nspid);
1334			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclobjtype - 1] = CharGetDatum(objtype);
1335			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclacl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
1336
1337			newtuple = heap_form_tuple(RelationGetDescr(rel), values, nulls);
1338			CatalogTupleInsert(rel, newtuple);
1339		}
1340		else
1341		{
1342			defAclOid = ((Form_pg_default_acl) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->oid;
1343
1344			/* update existing entry */
1345			values[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclacl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
1346			replaces[Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclacl - 1] = true;
1347
1348			newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(rel),
1349										 values, nulls, replaces);
1350			CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
1351		}
1352
1353		/* these dependencies don't change in an update */
1354		if (isNew)
1355		{
1356			/* dependency on role */
1357			recordDependencyOnOwner(DefaultAclRelationId, defAclOid,
1358									iacls->roleid);
1359
1360			/* dependency on namespace */
1361			if (OidIsValid(iacls->nspid))
1362			{
1363				ObjectAddress myself,
1364							referenced;
1365
1366				myself.classId = DefaultAclRelationId;
1367				myself.objectId = defAclOid;
1368				myself.objectSubId = 0;
1369
1370				referenced.classId = NamespaceRelationId;
1371				referenced.objectId = iacls->nspid;
1372				referenced.objectSubId = 0;
1373
1374				recordDependencyOn(&myself, &referenced, DEPENDENCY_AUTO);
1375			}
1376		}
1377
1378		/*
1379		 * Update the shared dependency ACL info
1380		 */
1381		nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
1382
1383		updateAclDependencies(DefaultAclRelationId,
1384							  defAclOid, 0,
1385							  iacls->roleid,
1386							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
1387							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
1388
1389		if (isNew)
1390			InvokeObjectPostCreateHook(DefaultAclRelationId, defAclOid, 0);
1391		else
1392			InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(DefaultAclRelationId, defAclOid, 0);
1393	}
1394
1395	if (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
1396		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
1397
1398	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
1399
1400	/* prevent error when processing duplicate objects */
1401	CommandCounterIncrement();
1402}
1403
1404
1405/*
1406 * RemoveRoleFromObjectACL
1407 *
1408 * Used by shdepDropOwned to remove mentions of a role in ACLs.
1409 *
1410 * Notice that this doesn't accept an objsubid parameter, which is a bit bogus
1411 * since the pg_shdepend record that caused us to call it certainly had one.
1412 * If, for example, pg_shdepend records the existence of a permission on
1413 * mytable.mycol, this function will effectively issue a REVOKE ALL ON TABLE
1414 * mytable.  That gets the job done because (per SQL spec) such a REVOKE also
1415 * revokes per-column permissions.  We could not recreate a situation where
1416 * the role has table-level but not column-level permissions; but it's okay
1417 * (for now anyway) because this is only used when we're dropping the role
1418 * and so all its permissions everywhere must go away.  At worst it's a bit
1419 * inefficient if the role has column permissions on several columns of the
1420 * same table.
1421 */
1422void
1423RemoveRoleFromObjectACL(Oid roleid, Oid classid, Oid objid)
1424{
1425	if (classid == DefaultAclRelationId)
1426	{
1427		InternalDefaultACL iacls;
1428		Form_pg_default_acl pg_default_acl_tuple;
1429		Relation	rel;
1430		ScanKeyData skey[1];
1431		SysScanDesc scan;
1432		HeapTuple	tuple;
1433
1434		/* first fetch info needed by SetDefaultACL */
1435		rel = table_open(DefaultAclRelationId, AccessShareLock);
1436
1437		ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
1438					Anum_pg_default_acl_oid,
1439					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
1440					ObjectIdGetDatum(objid));
1441
1442		scan = systable_beginscan(rel, DefaultAclOidIndexId, true,
1443								  NULL, 1, skey);
1444
1445		tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
1446
1447		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
1448			elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for default ACL %u", objid);
1449
1450		pg_default_acl_tuple = (Form_pg_default_acl) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
1451
1452		iacls.roleid = pg_default_acl_tuple->defaclrole;
1453		iacls.nspid = pg_default_acl_tuple->defaclnamespace;
1454
1455		switch (pg_default_acl_tuple->defaclobjtype)
1456		{
1457			case DEFACLOBJ_RELATION:
1458				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
1459				break;
1460			case DEFACLOBJ_SEQUENCE:
1461				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_SEQUENCE;
1462				break;
1463			case DEFACLOBJ_FUNCTION:
1464				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
1465				break;
1466			case DEFACLOBJ_TYPE:
1467				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_TYPE;
1468				break;
1469			case DEFACLOBJ_NAMESPACE:
1470				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_SCHEMA;
1471				break;
1472			case DEFACLOBJ_LARGEOBJECT:
1473				iacls.objtype = OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT;
1474				break;
1475			default:
1476				/* Shouldn't get here */
1477				elog(ERROR, "unexpected default ACL type: %d",
1478					 (int) pg_default_acl_tuple->defaclobjtype);
1479				break;
1480		}
1481
1482		systable_endscan(scan);
1483		table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
1484
1485		iacls.is_grant = false;
1486		iacls.all_privs = true;
1487		iacls.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
1488		iacls.grantees = list_make1_oid(roleid);
1489		iacls.grant_option = false;
1490		iacls.behavior = DROP_CASCADE;
1491
1492		/* Do it */
1493		SetDefaultACL(&iacls);
1494	}
1495	else
1496	{
1497		InternalGrant istmt;
1498
1499		switch (classid)
1500		{
1501			case RelationRelationId:
1502				/* it's OK to use TABLE for a sequence */
1503				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
1504				break;
1505			case DatabaseRelationId:
1506				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_DATABASE;
1507				break;
1508			case TypeRelationId:
1509				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_TYPE;
1510				break;
1511			case ProcedureRelationId:
1512				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
1513				break;
1514			case LanguageRelationId:
1515				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_LANGUAGE;
1516				break;
1517			case LargeObjectRelationId:
1518				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT;
1519				break;
1520			case NamespaceRelationId:
1521				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_SCHEMA;
1522				break;
1523			case TableSpaceRelationId:
1524				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_TABLESPACE;
1525				break;
1526			case ForeignServerRelationId:
1527				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER;
1528				break;
1529			case ForeignDataWrapperRelationId:
1530				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_FDW;
1531				break;
1532			case ParameterAclRelationId:
1533				istmt.objtype = OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL;
1534				break;
1535			default:
1536				elog(ERROR, "unexpected object class %u", classid);
1537				break;
1538		}
1539		istmt.is_grant = false;
1540		istmt.objects = list_make1_oid(objid);
1541		istmt.all_privs = true;
1542		istmt.privileges = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
1543		istmt.col_privs = NIL;
1544		istmt.grantees = list_make1_oid(roleid);
1545		istmt.grant_option = false;
1546		istmt.behavior = DROP_CASCADE;
1547
1548		ExecGrantStmt_oids(&istmt);
1549	}
1550}
1551
1552
1553/*
1554 * expand_col_privileges
1555 *
1556 * OR the specified privilege(s) into per-column array entries for each
1557 * specified attribute.  The per-column array is indexed starting at
1558 * FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber, up to relation's last attribute.
1559 */
1560static void
1561expand_col_privileges(List *colnames, Oid table_oid,
1562					  AclMode this_privileges,
1563					  AclMode *col_privileges,
1564					  int num_col_privileges)
1565{
1566	ListCell   *cell;
1567
1568	foreach(cell, colnames)
1569	{
1570		char	   *colname = strVal(lfirst(cell));
1571		AttrNumber	attnum;
1572
1573		attnum = get_attnum(table_oid, colname);
1574		if (attnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
1575			ereport(ERROR,
1576					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
1577					 errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
1578							colname, get_rel_name(table_oid))));
1579		attnum -= FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
1580		if (attnum <= 0 || attnum >= num_col_privileges)
1581			elog(ERROR, "column number out of range");	/* safety check */
1582		col_privileges[attnum] |= this_privileges;
1583	}
1584}
1585
1586/*
1587 * expand_all_col_privileges
1588 *
1589 * OR the specified privilege(s) into per-column array entries for each valid
1590 * attribute of a relation.  The per-column array is indexed starting at
1591 * FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber, up to relation's last attribute.
1592 */
1593static void
1594expand_all_col_privileges(Oid table_oid, Form_pg_class classForm,
1595						  AclMode this_privileges,
1596						  AclMode *col_privileges,
1597						  int num_col_privileges)
1598{
1599	AttrNumber	curr_att;
1600
1601	Assert(classForm->relnatts - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber < num_col_privileges);
1602	for (curr_att = FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber + 1;
1603		 curr_att <= classForm->relnatts;
1604		 curr_att++)
1605	{
1606		HeapTuple	attTuple;
1607		bool		isdropped;
1608
1609		if (curr_att == InvalidAttrNumber)
1610			continue;
1611
1612		/* Views don't have any system columns at all */
1613		if (classForm->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW && curr_att < 0)
1614			continue;
1615
1616		attTuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
1617								   ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid),
1618								   Int16GetDatum(curr_att));
1619		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attTuple))
1620			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for attribute %d of relation %u",
1621				 curr_att, table_oid);
1622
1623		isdropped = ((Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(attTuple))->attisdropped;
1624
1625		ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
1626
1627		/* ignore dropped columns */
1628		if (isdropped)
1629			continue;
1630
1631		col_privileges[curr_att - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber] |= this_privileges;
1632	}
1633}
1634
1635/*
1636 *	This processes attributes, but expects to be called from
1637 *	ExecGrant_Relation, not directly from ExecuteGrantStmt.
1638 */
1639static void
1640ExecGrant_Attribute(InternalGrant *istmt, Oid relOid, const char *relname,
1641					AttrNumber attnum, Oid ownerId, AclMode col_privileges,
1642					Relation attRelation, const Acl *old_rel_acl)
1643{
1644	HeapTuple	attr_tuple;
1645	Form_pg_attribute pg_attribute_tuple;
1646	Acl		   *old_acl;
1647	Acl		   *new_acl;
1648	Acl		   *merged_acl;
1649	Datum		aclDatum;
1650	bool		isNull;
1651	Oid			grantorId;
1652	AclMode		avail_goptions;
1653	bool		need_update;
1654	HeapTuple	newtuple;
1655	Datum		values[Natts_pg_attribute] = {0};
1656	bool		nulls[Natts_pg_attribute] = {0};
1657	bool		replaces[Natts_pg_attribute] = {0};
1658	int			noldmembers;
1659	int			nnewmembers;
1660	Oid		   *oldmembers;
1661	Oid		   *newmembers;
1662
1663	attr_tuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
1664								 ObjectIdGetDatum(relOid),
1665								 Int16GetDatum(attnum));
1666	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attr_tuple))
1667		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for attribute %d of relation %u",
1668			 attnum, relOid);
1669	pg_attribute_tuple = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(attr_tuple);
1670
1671	/*
1672	 * Get working copy of existing ACL. If there's no ACL, substitute the
1673	 * proper default.
1674	 */
1675	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(ATTNUM, attr_tuple, Anum_pg_attribute_attacl,
1676							   &isNull);
1677	if (isNull)
1678	{
1679		old_acl = acldefault(OBJECT_COLUMN, ownerId);
1680		/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
1681		noldmembers = 0;
1682		oldmembers = NULL;
1683	}
1684	else
1685	{
1686		old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
1687		/* Get the roles mentioned in the existing ACL */
1688		noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
1689	}
1690
1691	/*
1692	 * In select_best_grantor we should consider existing table-level ACL bits
1693	 * as well as the per-column ACL.  Build a new ACL that is their
1694	 * concatenation.  (This is a bit cheap and dirty compared to merging them
1695	 * properly with no duplications, but it's all we need here.)
1696	 */
1697	merged_acl = aclconcat(old_rel_acl, old_acl);
1698
1699	/* Determine ID to do the grant as, and available grant options */
1700	select_best_grantor(GetUserId(), col_privileges,
1701						merged_acl, ownerId,
1702						&grantorId, &avail_goptions);
1703
1704	pfree(merged_acl);
1705
1706	/*
1707	 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit the
1708	 * standards-mandated warning and error messages.  Note: we don't track
1709	 * whether the user actually used the ALL PRIVILEGES(columns) syntax for
1710	 * each column; we just approximate it by whether all the possible
1711	 * privileges are specified now.  Since the all_privs flag only determines
1712	 * whether a warning is issued, this seems close enough.
1713	 */
1714	col_privileges =
1715		restrict_and_check_grant(istmt->is_grant, avail_goptions,
1716								 (col_privileges == ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN),
1717								 col_privileges,
1718								 relOid, grantorId, OBJECT_COLUMN,
1719								 relname, attnum,
1720								 NameStr(pg_attribute_tuple->attname));
1721
1722	/*
1723	 * Generate new ACL.
1724	 */
1725	new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl, istmt->is_grant,
1726								   istmt->grant_option,
1727								   istmt->behavior, istmt->grantees,
1728								   col_privileges, grantorId,
1729								   ownerId);
1730
1731	/*
1732	 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
1733	 * shared dependency information.
1734	 */
1735	nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
1736
1737	/* finished building new ACL value, now insert it */
1738
1739	/*
1740	 * If the updated ACL is empty, we can set attacl to null, and maybe even
1741	 * avoid an update of the pg_attribute row.  This is worth testing because
1742	 * we'll come through here multiple times for any relation-level REVOKE,
1743	 * even if there were never any column GRANTs.  Note we are assuming that
1744	 * the "default" ACL state for columns is empty.
1745	 */
1746	if (ACL_NUM(new_acl) > 0)
1747	{
1748		values[Anum_pg_attribute_attacl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
1749		need_update = true;
1750	}
1751	else
1752	{
1753		nulls[Anum_pg_attribute_attacl - 1] = true;
1754		need_update = !isNull;
1755	}
1756	replaces[Anum_pg_attribute_attacl - 1] = true;
1757
1758	if (need_update)
1759	{
1760		newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(attr_tuple, RelationGetDescr(attRelation),
1761									 values, nulls, replaces);
1762
1763		CatalogTupleUpdate(attRelation, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
1764
1765		/* Update initial privileges for extensions */
1766		recordExtensionInitPriv(relOid, RelationRelationId, attnum,
1767								ACL_NUM(new_acl) > 0 ? new_acl : NULL);
1768
1769		/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
1770		updateAclDependencies(RelationRelationId, relOid, attnum,
1771							  ownerId,
1772							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
1773							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
1774	}
1775
1776	pfree(new_acl);
1777
1778	ReleaseSysCache(attr_tuple);
1779}
1780
1781/*
1782 *	This processes both sequences and non-sequences.
1783 */
1784static void
1785ExecGrant_Relation(InternalGrant *istmt)
1786{
1787	Relation	relation;
1788	Relation	attRelation;
1789	ListCell   *cell;
1790
1791	relation = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
1792	attRelation = table_open(AttributeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
1793
1794	foreach(cell, istmt->objects)
1795	{
1796		Oid			relOid = lfirst_oid(cell);
1797		Datum		aclDatum;
1798		Form_pg_class pg_class_tuple;
1799		bool		isNull;
1800		AclMode		this_privileges;
1801		AclMode    *col_privileges;
1802		int			num_col_privileges;
1803		bool		have_col_privileges;
1804		Acl		   *old_acl;
1805		Acl		   *old_rel_acl;
1806		int			noldmembers;
1807		Oid		   *oldmembers;
1808		Oid			ownerId;
1809		HeapTuple	tuple;
1810		ListCell   *cell_colprivs;
1811
1812		tuple = SearchSysCacheLocked1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(relOid));
1813		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
1814			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u", relOid);
1815		pg_class_tuple = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
1816
1817		/* Not sensible to grant on an index */
1818		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX ||
1819			pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX)
1820			ereport(ERROR,
1821					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
1822					 errmsg("\"%s\" is an index",
1823							NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname))));
1824
1825		/* Composite types aren't tables either */
1826		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
1827			ereport(ERROR,
1828					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
1829					 errmsg("\"%s\" is a composite type",
1830							NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname))));
1831
1832		/* Used GRANT SEQUENCE on a non-sequence? */
1833		if (istmt->objtype == OBJECT_SEQUENCE &&
1834			pg_class_tuple->relkind != RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
1835			ereport(ERROR,
1836					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
1837					 errmsg("\"%s\" is not a sequence",
1838							NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname))));
1839
1840		/* Adjust the default permissions based on object type */
1841		if (istmt->all_privs && istmt->privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1842		{
1843			if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
1844				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
1845			else
1846				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION;
1847		}
1848		else
1849			this_privileges = istmt->privileges;
1850
1851		/*
1852		 * The GRANT TABLE syntax can be used for sequences and non-sequences,
1853		 * so we have to look at the relkind to determine the supported
1854		 * permissions.  The OR of table and sequence permissions were already
1855		 * checked.
1856		 */
1857		if (istmt->objtype == OBJECT_TABLE)
1858		{
1859			if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
1860			{
1861				/*
1862				 * For backward compatibility, just throw a warning for
1863				 * invalid sequence permissions when using the non-sequence
1864				 * GRANT syntax.
1865				 */
1866				if (this_privileges & ~((AclMode) ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE))
1867				{
1868					/*
1869					 * Mention the object name because the user needs to know
1870					 * which operations succeeded.  This is required because
1871					 * WARNING allows the command to continue.
1872					 */
1873					ereport(WARNING,
1874							(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1875							 errmsg("sequence \"%s\" only supports USAGE, SELECT, and UPDATE privileges",
1876									NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname))));
1877					this_privileges &= (AclMode) ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SEQUENCE;
1878				}
1879			}
1880			else
1881			{
1882				if (this_privileges & ~((AclMode) ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_RELATION))
1883				{
1884					/*
1885					 * USAGE is the only permission supported by sequences but
1886					 * not by non-sequences.  Don't mention the object name
1887					 * because we didn't in the combined TABLE | SEQUENCE
1888					 * check.
1889					 */
1890					ereport(ERROR,
1891							(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
1892							 errmsg("invalid privilege type %s for table",
1893									"USAGE")));
1894				}
1895			}
1896		}
1897
1898		/*
1899		 * Set up array in which we'll accumulate any column privilege bits
1900		 * that need modification.  The array is indexed such that entry [0]
1901		 * corresponds to FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber.
1902		 */
1903		num_col_privileges = pg_class_tuple->relnatts - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber + 1;
1904		col_privileges = (AclMode *) palloc0(num_col_privileges * sizeof(AclMode));
1905		have_col_privileges = false;
1906
1907		/*
1908		 * If we are revoking relation privileges that are also column
1909		 * privileges, we must implicitly revoke them from each column too,
1910		 * per SQL spec.  (We don't need to implicitly add column privileges
1911		 * during GRANT because the permissions-checking code always checks
1912		 * both relation and per-column privileges.)
1913		 */
1914		if (!istmt->is_grant &&
1915			(this_privileges & ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN) != 0)
1916		{
1917			expand_all_col_privileges(relOid, pg_class_tuple,
1918									  this_privileges & ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN,
1919									  col_privileges,
1920									  num_col_privileges);
1921			have_col_privileges = true;
1922		}
1923
1924		/*
1925		 * Get owner ID and working copy of existing ACL. If there's no ACL,
1926		 * substitute the proper default.
1927		 */
1928		ownerId = pg_class_tuple->relowner;
1929		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(RELOID, tuple, Anum_pg_class_relacl,
1930								   &isNull);
1931		if (isNull)
1932		{
1933			switch (pg_class_tuple->relkind)
1934			{
1935				case RELKIND_SEQUENCE:
1936					old_acl = acldefault(OBJECT_SEQUENCE, ownerId);
1937					break;
1938				default:
1939					old_acl = acldefault(OBJECT_TABLE, ownerId);
1940					break;
1941			}
1942			/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
1943			noldmembers = 0;
1944			oldmembers = NULL;
1945		}
1946		else
1947		{
1948			old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
1949			/* Get the roles mentioned in the existing ACL */
1950			noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
1951		}
1952
1953		/* Need an extra copy of original rel ACL for column handling */
1954		old_rel_acl = aclcopy(old_acl);
1955
1956		/*
1957		 * Handle relation-level privileges, if any were specified
1958		 */
1959		if (this_privileges != ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
1960		{
1961			AclMode		avail_goptions;
1962			Acl		   *new_acl;
1963			Oid			grantorId;
1964			HeapTuple	newtuple;
1965			Datum		values[Natts_pg_class] = {0};
1966			bool		nulls[Natts_pg_class] = {0};
1967			bool		replaces[Natts_pg_class] = {0};
1968			int			nnewmembers;
1969			Oid		   *newmembers;
1970			ObjectType	objtype;
1971
1972			/* Determine ID to do the grant as, and available grant options */
1973			select_best_grantor(GetUserId(), this_privileges,
1974								old_acl, ownerId,
1975								&grantorId, &avail_goptions);
1976
1977			switch (pg_class_tuple->relkind)
1978			{
1979				case RELKIND_SEQUENCE:
1980					objtype = OBJECT_SEQUENCE;
1981					break;
1982				default:
1983					objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
1984					break;
1985			}
1986
1987			/*
1988			 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit
1989			 * the standards-mandated warning and error messages.
1990			 */
1991			this_privileges =
1992				restrict_and_check_grant(istmt->is_grant, avail_goptions,
1993										 istmt->all_privs, this_privileges,
1994										 relOid, grantorId, objtype,
1995										 NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname),
1996										 0, NULL);
1997
1998			/*
1999			 * Generate new ACL.
2000			 */
2001			new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl,
2002										   istmt->is_grant,
2003										   istmt->grant_option,
2004										   istmt->behavior,
2005										   istmt->grantees,
2006										   this_privileges,
2007										   grantorId,
2008										   ownerId);
2009
2010			/*
2011			 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct
2012			 * the shared dependency information.
2013			 */
2014			nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
2015
2016			/* finished building new ACL value, now insert it */
2017			replaces[Anum_pg_class_relacl - 1] = true;
2018			values[Anum_pg_class_relacl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
2019
2020			newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(relation),
2021										 values, nulls, replaces);
2022
2023			CatalogTupleUpdate(relation, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
2024			UnlockTuple(relation, &tuple->t_self, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
2025
2026			/* Update initial privileges for extensions */
2027			recordExtensionInitPriv(relOid, RelationRelationId, 0, new_acl);
2028
2029			/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
2030			updateAclDependencies(RelationRelationId, relOid, 0,
2031								  ownerId,
2032								  noldmembers, oldmembers,
2033								  nnewmembers, newmembers);
2034
2035			pfree(new_acl);
2036		}
2037		else
2038			UnlockTuple(relation, &tuple->t_self, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
2039
2040		/*
2041		 * Handle column-level privileges, if any were specified or implied.
2042		 * We first expand the user-specified column privileges into the
2043		 * array, and then iterate over all nonempty array entries.
2044		 */
2045		foreach(cell_colprivs, istmt->col_privs)
2046		{
2047			AccessPriv *col_privs = (AccessPriv *) lfirst(cell_colprivs);
2048
2049			if (col_privs->priv_name == NULL)
2050				this_privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN;
2051			else
2052				this_privileges = string_to_privilege(col_privs->priv_name);
2053
2054			if (this_privileges & ~((AclMode) ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_COLUMN))
2055				ereport(ERROR,
2056						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
2057						 errmsg("invalid privilege type %s for column",
2058								privilege_to_string(this_privileges))));
2059
2060			if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE &&
2061				this_privileges & ~((AclMode) ACL_SELECT))
2062			{
2063				/*
2064				 * The only column privilege allowed on sequences is SELECT.
2065				 * This is a warning not error because we do it that way for
2066				 * relation-level privileges.
2067				 */
2068				ereport(WARNING,
2069						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
2070						 errmsg("sequence \"%s\" only supports SELECT column privileges",
2071								NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname))));
2072
2073				this_privileges &= (AclMode) ACL_SELECT;
2074			}
2075
2076			expand_col_privileges(col_privs->cols, relOid,
2077								  this_privileges,
2078								  col_privileges,
2079								  num_col_privileges);
2080			have_col_privileges = true;
2081		}
2082
2083		if (have_col_privileges)
2084		{
2085			AttrNumber	i;
2086
2087			for (i = 0; i < num_col_privileges; i++)
2088			{
2089				if (col_privileges[i] == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
2090					continue;
2091				ExecGrant_Attribute(istmt,
2092									relOid,
2093									NameStr(pg_class_tuple->relname),
2094									i + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,
2095									ownerId,
2096									col_privileges[i],
2097									attRelation,
2098									old_rel_acl);
2099			}
2100		}
2101
2102		pfree(old_rel_acl);
2103		pfree(col_privileges);
2104
2105		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
2106
2107		/* prevent error when processing duplicate objects */
2108		CommandCounterIncrement();
2109	}
2110
2111	table_close(attRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
2112	table_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
2113}
2114
2115static void
2116ExecGrant_common(InternalGrant *istmt, Oid classid, AclMode default_privs,
2117				 void (*object_check) (InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple))
2118{
2119	int			cacheid;
2120	Relation	relation;
2121	ListCell   *cell;
2122
2123	if (istmt->all_privs && istmt->privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
2124		istmt->privileges = default_privs;
2125
2126	cacheid = get_object_catcache_oid(classid);
2127
2128	relation = table_open(classid, RowExclusiveLock);
2129
2130	foreach(cell, istmt->objects)
2131	{
2132		Oid			objectid = lfirst_oid(cell);
2133		Datum		aclDatum;
2134		Datum		nameDatum;
2135		bool		isNull;
2136		AclMode		avail_goptions;
2137		AclMode		this_privileges;
2138		Acl		   *old_acl;
2139		Acl		   *new_acl;
2140		Oid			grantorId;
2141		Oid			ownerId;
2142		HeapTuple	tuple;
2143		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2144		Datum	   *values = palloc0_array(Datum, RelationGetDescr(relation)->natts);
2145		bool	   *nulls = palloc0_array(bool, RelationGetDescr(relation)->natts);
2146		bool	   *replaces = palloc0_array(bool, RelationGetDescr(relation)->natts);
2147		int			noldmembers;
2148		int			nnewmembers;
2149		Oid		   *oldmembers;
2150		Oid		   *newmembers;
2151
2152		tuple = SearchSysCacheLocked1(cacheid, ObjectIdGetDatum(objectid));
2153		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
2154			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for %s %u", get_object_class_descr(classid), objectid);
2155
2156		/*
2157		 * Additional object-type-specific checks
2158		 */
2159		if (object_check)
2160			object_check(istmt, tuple);
2161
2162		/*
2163		 * Get owner ID and working copy of existing ACL. If there's no ACL,
2164		 * substitute the proper default.
2165		 */
2166		ownerId = DatumGetObjectId(SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(cacheid,
2167														  tuple,
2168														  get_object_attnum_owner(classid)));
2169		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(cacheid,
2170								   tuple,
2171								   get_object_attnum_acl(classid),
2172								   &isNull);
2173		if (isNull)
2174		{
2175			old_acl = acldefault(get_object_type(classid, objectid), ownerId);
2176			/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
2177			noldmembers = 0;
2178			oldmembers = NULL;
2179		}
2180		else
2181		{
2182			old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
2183			/* Get the roles mentioned in the existing ACL */
2184			noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
2185		}
2186
2187		/* Determine ID to do the grant as, and available grant options */
2188		select_best_grantor(GetUserId(), istmt->privileges,
2189							old_acl, ownerId,
2190							&grantorId, &avail_goptions);
2191
2192		nameDatum = SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(cacheid, tuple,
2193										   get_object_attnum_name(classid));
2194
2195		/*
2196		 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit the
2197		 * standards-mandated warning and error messages.
2198		 */
2199		this_privileges =
2200			restrict_and_check_grant(istmt->is_grant, avail_goptions,
2201									 istmt->all_privs, istmt->privileges,
2202									 objectid, grantorId, get_object_type(classid, objectid),
2203									 NameStr(*DatumGetName(nameDatum)),
2204									 0, NULL);
2205
2206		/*
2207		 * Generate new ACL.
2208		 */
2209		new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl, istmt->is_grant,
2210									   istmt->grant_option, istmt->behavior,
2211									   istmt->grantees, this_privileges,
2212									   grantorId, ownerId);
2213
2214		/*
2215		 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
2216		 * shared dependency information.
2217		 */
2218		nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
2219
2220		/* finished building new ACL value, now insert it */
2221		replaces[get_object_attnum_acl(classid) - 1] = true;
2222		values[get_object_attnum_acl(classid) - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
2223
2224		newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(relation), values,
2225									 nulls, replaces);
2226
2227		CatalogTupleUpdate(relation, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
2228		UnlockTuple(relation, &tuple->t_self, InplaceUpdateTupleLock);
2229
2230		/* Update initial privileges for extensions */
2231		recordExtensionInitPriv(objectid, classid, 0, new_acl);
2232
2233		/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
2234		updateAclDependencies(classid,
2235							  objectid, 0,
2236							  ownerId,
2237							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
2238							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
2239
2240		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
2241
2242		pfree(new_acl);
2243
2244		/* prevent error when processing duplicate objects */
2245		CommandCounterIncrement();
2246	}
2247
2248	table_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
2249}
2250
2251static void
2252ExecGrant_Language_check(InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple)
2253{
2254	Form_pg_language pg_language_tuple;
2255
2256	pg_language_tuple = (Form_pg_language) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
2257
2258	if (!pg_language_tuple->lanpltrusted)
2259		ereport(ERROR,
2260				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2261				 errmsg("language \"%s\" is not trusted",
2262						NameStr(pg_language_tuple->lanname)),
2263				 errdetail("GRANT and REVOKE are not allowed on untrusted languages, "
2264						   "because only superusers can use untrusted languages.")));
2265}
2266
2267static void
2268ExecGrant_Largeobject(InternalGrant *istmt)
2269{
2270	Relation	relation;
2271	ListCell   *cell;
2272
2273	if (istmt->all_privs && istmt->privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
2274		istmt->privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_LARGEOBJECT;
2275
2276	relation = table_open(LargeObjectMetadataRelationId,
2277						  RowExclusiveLock);
2278
2279	foreach(cell, istmt->objects)
2280	{
2281		Oid			loid = lfirst_oid(cell);
2282		Form_pg_largeobject_metadata form_lo_meta;
2283		char		loname[NAMEDATALEN];
2284		Datum		aclDatum;
2285		bool		isNull;
2286		AclMode		avail_goptions;
2287		AclMode		this_privileges;
2288		Acl		   *old_acl;
2289		Acl		   *new_acl;
2290		Oid			grantorId;
2291		Oid			ownerId;
2292		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2293		Datum		values[Natts_pg_largeobject_metadata] = {0};
2294		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_largeobject_metadata] = {0};
2295		bool		replaces[Natts_pg_largeobject_metadata] = {0};
2296		int			noldmembers;
2297		int			nnewmembers;
2298		Oid		   *oldmembers;
2299		Oid		   *newmembers;
2300		ScanKeyData entry[1];
2301		SysScanDesc scan;
2302		HeapTuple	tuple;
2303
2304		/* There's no syscache for pg_largeobject_metadata */
2305		ScanKeyInit(&entry[0],
2306					Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_oid,
2307					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2308					ObjectIdGetDatum(loid));
2309
2310		scan = systable_beginscan(relation,
2311								  LargeObjectMetadataOidIndexId, true,
2312								  NULL, 1, entry);
2313
2314		tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
2315		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
2316			elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for large object %u", loid);
2317
2318		form_lo_meta = (Form_pg_largeobject_metadata) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
2319
2320		/*
2321		 * Get owner ID and working copy of existing ACL. If there's no ACL,
2322		 * substitute the proper default.
2323		 */
2324		ownerId = form_lo_meta->lomowner;
2325		aclDatum = heap_getattr(tuple,
2326								Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_lomacl,
2327								RelationGetDescr(relation), &isNull);
2328		if (isNull)
2329		{
2330			old_acl = acldefault(OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT, ownerId);
2331			/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
2332			noldmembers = 0;
2333			oldmembers = NULL;
2334		}
2335		else
2336		{
2337			old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
2338			/* Get the roles mentioned in the existing ACL */
2339			noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
2340		}
2341
2342		/* Determine ID to do the grant as, and available grant options */
2343		select_best_grantor(GetUserId(), istmt->privileges,
2344							old_acl, ownerId,
2345							&grantorId, &avail_goptions);
2346
2347		/*
2348		 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit the
2349		 * standards-mandated warning and error messages.
2350		 */
2351		snprintf(loname, sizeof(loname), "large object %u", loid);
2352		this_privileges =
2353			restrict_and_check_grant(istmt->is_grant, avail_goptions,
2354									 istmt->all_privs, istmt->privileges,
2355									 loid, grantorId, OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT,
2356									 loname, 0, NULL);
2357
2358		/*
2359		 * Generate new ACL.
2360		 */
2361		new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl, istmt->is_grant,
2362									   istmt->grant_option, istmt->behavior,
2363									   istmt->grantees, this_privileges,
2364									   grantorId, ownerId);
2365
2366		/*
2367		 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
2368		 * shared dependency information.
2369		 */
2370		nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
2371
2372		/* finished building new ACL value, now insert it */
2373		replaces[Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_lomacl - 1] = true;
2374		values[Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_lomacl - 1]
2375			= PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
2376
2377		newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(relation),
2378									 values, nulls, replaces);
2379
2380		CatalogTupleUpdate(relation, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
2381
2382		/* Update initial privileges for extensions */
2383		recordExtensionInitPriv(loid, LargeObjectRelationId, 0, new_acl);
2384
2385		/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
2386		updateAclDependencies(LargeObjectRelationId,
2387							  form_lo_meta->oid, 0,
2388							  ownerId,
2389							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
2390							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
2391
2392		systable_endscan(scan);
2393
2394		pfree(new_acl);
2395
2396		/* prevent error when processing duplicate objects */
2397		CommandCounterIncrement();
2398	}
2399
2400	table_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
2401}
2402
2403static void
2404ExecGrant_Type_check(InternalGrant *istmt, HeapTuple tuple)
2405{
2406	Form_pg_type pg_type_tuple;
2407
2408	pg_type_tuple = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
2409
2410	/* Disallow GRANT on dependent types */
2411	if (IsTrueArrayType(pg_type_tuple))
2412		ereport(ERROR,
2413				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
2414				 errmsg("cannot set privileges of array types"),
2415				 errhint("Set the privileges of the element type instead.")));
2416	if (pg_type_tuple->typtype == TYPTYPE_MULTIRANGE)
2417		ereport(ERROR,
2418				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_GRANT_OPERATION),
2419				 errmsg("cannot set privileges of multirange types"),
2420				 errhint("Set the privileges of the range type instead.")));
2421}
2422
2423static void
2424ExecGrant_Parameter(InternalGrant *istmt)
2425{
2426	Relation	relation;
2427	ListCell   *cell;
2428
2429	if (istmt->all_privs && istmt->privileges == ACL_NO_RIGHTS)
2430		istmt->privileges = ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_PARAMETER_ACL;
2431
2432	relation = table_open(ParameterAclRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2433
2434	foreach(cell, istmt->objects)
2435	{
2436		Oid			parameterId = lfirst_oid(cell);
2437		Datum		nameDatum;
2438		const char *parname;
2439		Datum		aclDatum;
2440		bool		isNull;
2441		AclMode		avail_goptions;
2442		AclMode		this_privileges;
2443		Acl		   *old_acl;
2444		Acl		   *new_acl;
2445		Oid			grantorId;
2446		Oid			ownerId;
2447		HeapTuple	tuple;
2448		int			noldmembers;
2449		int			nnewmembers;
2450		Oid		   *oldmembers;
2451		Oid		   *newmembers;
2452
2453		tuple = SearchSysCache1(PARAMETERACLOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(parameterId));
2454		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
2455			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for parameter ACL %u",
2456				 parameterId);
2457
2458		/* We'll need the GUC's name */
