2459		nameDatum = SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(PARAMETERACLOID, tuple,
2460										   Anum_pg_parameter_acl_parname);
2461		parname = TextDatumGetCString(nameDatum);
2462
2463		/* Treat all parameters as belonging to the bootstrap superuser. */
2464		ownerId = BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID;
2465
2466		/*
2467		 * Get working copy of existing ACL. If there's no ACL, substitute the
2468		 * proper default.
2469		 */
2470		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(PARAMETERACLOID, tuple,
2471								   Anum_pg_parameter_acl_paracl,
2472								   &isNull);
2473
2474		if (isNull)
2475		{
2476			old_acl = acldefault(istmt->objtype, ownerId);
2477			/* There are no old member roles according to the catalogs */
2478			noldmembers = 0;
2479			oldmembers = NULL;
2480		}
2481		else
2482		{
2483			old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
2484			/* Get the roles mentioned in the existing ACL */
2485			noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
2486		}
2487
2488		/* Determine ID to do the grant as, and available grant options */
2489		select_best_grantor(GetUserId(), istmt->privileges,
2490							old_acl, ownerId,
2491							&grantorId, &avail_goptions);
2492
2493		/*
2494		 * Restrict the privileges to what we can actually grant, and emit the
2495		 * standards-mandated warning and error messages.
2496		 */
2497		this_privileges =
2498			restrict_and_check_grant(istmt->is_grant, avail_goptions,
2499									 istmt->all_privs, istmt->privileges,
2500									 parameterId, grantorId,
2501									 OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL,
2502									 parname,
2503									 0, NULL);
2504
2505		/*
2506		 * Generate new ACL.
2507		 */
2508		new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl, istmt->is_grant,
2509									   istmt->grant_option, istmt->behavior,
2510									   istmt->grantees, this_privileges,
2511									   grantorId, ownerId);
2512
2513		/*
2514		 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
2515		 * shared dependency information.
2516		 */
2517		nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
2518
2519		/*
2520		 * If the new ACL is equal to the default, we don't need the catalog
2521		 * entry any longer.  Delete it rather than updating it, to avoid
2522		 * leaving a degenerate entry.
2523		 */
2524		if (aclequal(new_acl, acldefault(istmt->objtype, ownerId)))
2525		{
2526			CatalogTupleDelete(relation, &tuple->t_self);
2527		}
2528		else
2529		{
2530			/* finished building new ACL value, now insert it */
2531			HeapTuple	newtuple;
2532			Datum		values[Natts_pg_parameter_acl] = {0};
2533			bool		nulls[Natts_pg_parameter_acl] = {0};
2534			bool		replaces[Natts_pg_parameter_acl] = {0};
2535
2536			replaces[Anum_pg_parameter_acl_paracl - 1] = true;
2537			values[Anum_pg_parameter_acl_paracl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
2538
2539			newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(relation),
2540										 values, nulls, replaces);
2541
2542			CatalogTupleUpdate(relation, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
2543		}
2544
2545		/* Update initial privileges for extensions */
2546		recordExtensionInitPriv(parameterId, ParameterAclRelationId, 0,
2547								new_acl);
2548
2549		/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
2550		updateAclDependencies(ParameterAclRelationId, parameterId, 0,
2551							  ownerId,
2552							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
2553							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
2554
2555		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
2556		pfree(new_acl);
2557
2558		/* prevent error when processing duplicate objects */
2559		CommandCounterIncrement();
2560	}
2561
2562	table_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
2563}
2564
2565
2566static AclMode
2567string_to_privilege(const char *privname)
2568{
2569	if (strcmp(privname, "insert") == 0)
2570		return ACL_INSERT;
2571	if (strcmp(privname, "select") == 0)
2572		return ACL_SELECT;
2573	if (strcmp(privname, "update") == 0)
2574		return ACL_UPDATE;
2575	if (strcmp(privname, "delete") == 0)
2576		return ACL_DELETE;
2577	if (strcmp(privname, "truncate") == 0)
2578		return ACL_TRUNCATE;
2579	if (strcmp(privname, "references") == 0)
2580		return ACL_REFERENCES;
2581	if (strcmp(privname, "trigger") == 0)
2582		return ACL_TRIGGER;
2583	if (strcmp(privname, "execute") == 0)
2584		return ACL_EXECUTE;
2585	if (strcmp(privname, "usage") == 0)
2586		return ACL_USAGE;
2587	if (strcmp(privname, "create") == 0)
2588		return ACL_CREATE;
2589	if (strcmp(privname, "temporary") == 0)
2590		return ACL_CREATE_TEMP;
2591	if (strcmp(privname, "temp") == 0)
2592		return ACL_CREATE_TEMP;
2593	if (strcmp(privname, "connect") == 0)
2594		return ACL_CONNECT;
2595	if (strcmp(privname, "set") == 0)
2596		return ACL_SET;
2597	if (strcmp(privname, "alter system") == 0)
2598		return ACL_ALTER_SYSTEM;
2599	if (strcmp(privname, "maintain") == 0)
2600		return ACL_MAINTAIN;
2601	ereport(ERROR,
2602			(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2603			 errmsg("unrecognized privilege type \"%s\"", privname)));
2604	return 0;					/* appease compiler */
2605}
2606
2607static const char *
2608privilege_to_string(AclMode privilege)
2609{
2610	switch (privilege)
2611	{
2612		case ACL_INSERT:
2613			return "INSERT";
2614		case ACL_SELECT:
2615			return "SELECT";
2616		case ACL_UPDATE:
2617			return "UPDATE";
2618		case ACL_DELETE:
2619			return "DELETE";
2620		case ACL_TRUNCATE:
2621			return "TRUNCATE";
2622		case ACL_REFERENCES:
2623			return "REFERENCES";
2624		case ACL_TRIGGER:
2625			return "TRIGGER";
2626		case ACL_EXECUTE:
2627			return "EXECUTE";
2628		case ACL_USAGE:
2629			return "USAGE";
2630		case ACL_CREATE:
2631			return "CREATE";
2632		case ACL_CREATE_TEMP:
2633			return "TEMP";
2634		case ACL_CONNECT:
2635			return "CONNECT";
2636		case ACL_SET:
2637			return "SET";
2638		case ACL_ALTER_SYSTEM:
2639			return "ALTER SYSTEM";
2640		case ACL_MAINTAIN:
2641			return "MAINTAIN";
2642		default:
2643			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized privilege: %d", (int) privilege);
2644	}
2645	return NULL;				/* appease compiler */
2646}
2647
2648/*
2649 * Standardized reporting of aclcheck permissions failures.
2650 *
2651 * Note: we do not double-quote the %s's below, because many callers
2652 * supply strings that might be already quoted.
2653 */
2654void
2655aclcheck_error(AclResult aclerr, ObjectType objtype,
2656			   const char *objectname)
2657{
2658	switch (aclerr)
2659	{
2660		case ACLCHECK_OK:
2661			/* no error, so return to caller */
2662			break;
2663		case ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV:
2664			{
2665				const char *msg = "???";
2666
2667				switch (objtype)
2668				{
2669					case OBJECT_AGGREGATE:
2670						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for aggregate %s");
2671						break;
2672					case OBJECT_COLLATION:
2673						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for collation %s");
2674						break;
2675					case OBJECT_COLUMN:
2676						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for column %s");
2677						break;
2678					case OBJECT_CONVERSION:
2679						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for conversion %s");
2680						break;
2681					case OBJECT_DATABASE:
2682						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for database %s");
2683						break;
2684					case OBJECT_DOMAIN:
2685						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for domain %s");
2686						break;
2687					case OBJECT_EVENT_TRIGGER:
2688						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for event trigger %s");
2689						break;
2690					case OBJECT_EXTENSION:
2691						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for extension %s");
2692						break;
2693					case OBJECT_FDW:
2694						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for foreign-data wrapper %s");
2695						break;
2696					case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
2697						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for foreign server %s");
2698						break;
2699					case OBJECT_FOREIGN_TABLE:
2700						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for foreign table %s");
2701						break;
2702					case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
2703						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for function %s");
2704						break;
2705					case OBJECT_INDEX:
2706						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for index %s");
2707						break;
2708					case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
2709						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for language %s");
2710						break;
2711					case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
2712						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for large object %s");
2713						break;
2714					case OBJECT_MATVIEW:
2715						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for materialized view %s");
2716						break;
2717					case OBJECT_OPCLASS:
2718						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for operator class %s");
2719						break;
2720					case OBJECT_OPERATOR:
2721						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for operator %s");
2722						break;
2723					case OBJECT_OPFAMILY:
2724						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for operator family %s");
2725						break;
2726					case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
2727						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for parameter %s");
2728						break;
2729					case OBJECT_POLICY:
2730						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for policy %s");
2731						break;
2732					case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
2733						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for procedure %s");
2734						break;
2735					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION:
2736						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for publication %s");
2737						break;
2738					case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
2739						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for routine %s");
2740						break;
2741					case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
2742						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for schema %s");
2743						break;
2744					case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
2745						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for sequence %s");
2746						break;
2747					case OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT:
2748						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for statistics object %s");
2749						break;
2750					case OBJECT_SUBSCRIPTION:
2751						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for subscription %s");
2752						break;
2753					case OBJECT_TABLE:
2754						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for table %s");
2755						break;
2756					case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
2757						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for tablespace %s");
2758						break;
2759					case OBJECT_TSCONFIGURATION:
2760						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for text search configuration %s");
2761						break;
2762					case OBJECT_TSDICTIONARY:
2763						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for text search dictionary %s");
2764						break;
2765					case OBJECT_TYPE:
2766						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for type %s");
2767						break;
2768					case OBJECT_VIEW:
2769						msg = gettext_noop("permission denied for view %s");
2770						break;
2771						/* these currently aren't used */
2772					case OBJECT_ACCESS_METHOD:
2773					case OBJECT_AMOP:
2774					case OBJECT_AMPROC:
2775					case OBJECT_ATTRIBUTE:
2776					case OBJECT_CAST:
2777					case OBJECT_DEFAULT:
2778					case OBJECT_DEFACL:
2779					case OBJECT_DOMCONSTRAINT:
2780					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION_NAMESPACE:
2781					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION_REL:
2782					case OBJECT_ROLE:
2783					case OBJECT_RULE:
2784					case OBJECT_TABCONSTRAINT:
2785					case OBJECT_TRANSFORM:
2786					case OBJECT_TRIGGER:
2787					case OBJECT_TSPARSER:
2788					case OBJECT_TSTEMPLATE:
2789					case OBJECT_USER_MAPPING:
2790						elog(ERROR, "unsupported object type: %d", objtype);
2791				}
2792
2793				ereport(ERROR,
2794						(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
2795						 errmsg(msg, objectname)));
2796				break;
2797			}
2798		case ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER:
2799			{
2800				const char *msg = "???";
2801
2802				switch (objtype)
2803				{
2804					case OBJECT_AGGREGATE:
2805						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of aggregate %s");
2806						break;
2807					case OBJECT_COLLATION:
2808						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of collation %s");
2809						break;
2810					case OBJECT_CONVERSION:
2811						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of conversion %s");
2812						break;
2813					case OBJECT_DATABASE:
2814						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of database %s");
2815						break;
2816					case OBJECT_DOMAIN:
2817						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of domain %s");
2818						break;
2819					case OBJECT_EVENT_TRIGGER:
2820						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of event trigger %s");
2821						break;
2822					case OBJECT_EXTENSION:
2823						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of extension %s");
2824						break;
2825					case OBJECT_FDW:
2826						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of foreign-data wrapper %s");
2827						break;
2828					case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
2829						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of foreign server %s");
2830						break;
2831					case OBJECT_FOREIGN_TABLE:
2832						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of foreign table %s");
2833						break;
2834					case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
2835						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of function %s");
2836						break;
2837					case OBJECT_INDEX:
2838						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of index %s");
2839						break;
2840					case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
2841						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of language %s");
2842						break;
2843					case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
2844						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of large object %s");
2845						break;
2846					case OBJECT_MATVIEW:
2847						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of materialized view %s");
2848						break;
2849					case OBJECT_OPCLASS:
2850						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of operator class %s");
2851						break;
2852					case OBJECT_OPERATOR:
2853						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of operator %s");
2854						break;
2855					case OBJECT_OPFAMILY:
2856						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of operator family %s");
2857						break;
2858					case OBJECT_PROCEDURE:
2859						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of procedure %s");
2860						break;
2861					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION:
2862						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of publication %s");
2863						break;
2864					case OBJECT_ROUTINE:
2865						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of routine %s");
2866						break;
2867					case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
2868						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of sequence %s");
2869						break;
2870					case OBJECT_SUBSCRIPTION:
2871						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of subscription %s");
2872						break;
2873					case OBJECT_TABLE:
2874						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of table %s");
2875						break;
2876					case OBJECT_TYPE:
2877						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of type %s");
2878						break;
2879					case OBJECT_VIEW:
2880						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of view %s");
2881						break;
2882					case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
2883						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of schema %s");
2884						break;
2885					case OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT:
2886						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of statistics object %s");
2887						break;
2888					case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
2889						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of tablespace %s");
2890						break;
2891					case OBJECT_TSCONFIGURATION:
2892						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of text search configuration %s");
2893						break;
2894					case OBJECT_TSDICTIONARY:
2895						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of text search dictionary %s");
2896						break;
2897
2898						/*
2899						 * Special cases: For these, the error message talks
2900						 * about "relation", because that's where the
2901						 * ownership is attached.  See also
2902						 * check_object_ownership().
2903						 */
2904					case OBJECT_COLUMN:
2905					case OBJECT_POLICY:
2906					case OBJECT_RULE:
2907					case OBJECT_TABCONSTRAINT:
2908					case OBJECT_TRIGGER:
2909						msg = gettext_noop("must be owner of relation %s");
2910						break;
2911						/* these currently aren't used */
2912					case OBJECT_ACCESS_METHOD:
2913					case OBJECT_AMOP:
2914					case OBJECT_AMPROC:
2915					case OBJECT_ATTRIBUTE:
2916					case OBJECT_CAST:
2917					case OBJECT_DEFAULT:
2918					case OBJECT_DEFACL:
2919					case OBJECT_DOMCONSTRAINT:
2920					case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
2921					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION_NAMESPACE:
2922					case OBJECT_PUBLICATION_REL:
2923					case OBJECT_ROLE:
2924					case OBJECT_TRANSFORM:
2925					case OBJECT_TSPARSER:
2926					case OBJECT_TSTEMPLATE:
2927					case OBJECT_USER_MAPPING:
2928						elog(ERROR, "unsupported object type: %d", objtype);
2929				}
2930
2931				ereport(ERROR,
2932						(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
2933						 errmsg(msg, objectname)));
2934				break;
2935			}
2936		default:
2937			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized AclResult: %d", (int) aclerr);
2938			break;
2939	}
2940}
2941
2942
2943void
2944aclcheck_error_col(AclResult aclerr, ObjectType objtype,
2945				   const char *objectname, const char *colname)
2946{
2947	switch (aclerr)
2948	{
2949		case ACLCHECK_OK:
2950			/* no error, so return to caller */
2951			break;
2952		case ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV:
2953			ereport(ERROR,
2954					(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
2955					 errmsg("permission denied for column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\"",
2956							colname, objectname)));
2957			break;
2958		case ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER:
2959			/* relation msg is OK since columns don't have separate owners */
2960			aclcheck_error(aclerr, objtype, objectname);
2961			break;
2962		default:
2963			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized AclResult: %d", (int) aclerr);
2964			break;
2965	}
2966}
2967
2968
2969/*
2970 * Special common handling for types: use element type instead of array type,
2971 * and format nicely
2972 */
2973void
2974aclcheck_error_type(AclResult aclerr, Oid typeOid)
2975{
2976	Oid			element_type = get_element_type(typeOid);
2977
2978	aclcheck_error(aclerr, OBJECT_TYPE, format_type_be(element_type ? element_type : typeOid));
2979}
2980
2981
2982/*
2983 * Relay for the various pg_*_mask routines depending on object kind
2984 */
2985static AclMode
2986pg_aclmask(ObjectType objtype, Oid object_oid, AttrNumber attnum, Oid roleid,
2987		   AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
2988{
2989	switch (objtype)
2990	{
2991		case OBJECT_COLUMN:
2992			return
2993				pg_class_aclmask(object_oid, roleid, mask, how) |
2994				pg_attribute_aclmask(object_oid, attnum, roleid, mask, how);
2995		case OBJECT_TABLE:
2996		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
2997			return pg_class_aclmask(object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
2998		case OBJECT_DATABASE:
2999			return object_aclmask(DatabaseRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3000		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
3001			return object_aclmask(ProcedureRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3002		case OBJECT_LANGUAGE:
3003			return object_aclmask(LanguageRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3004		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
3005			return pg_largeobject_aclmask_snapshot(object_oid, roleid,
3006												   mask, how, NULL);
3007		case OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL:
3008			return pg_parameter_acl_aclmask(object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3009		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
3010			return object_aclmask(NamespaceRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3011		case OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT:
3012			elog(ERROR, "grantable rights not supported for statistics objects");
3013			/* not reached, but keep compiler quiet */
3014			return ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
3015		case OBJECT_TABLESPACE:
3016			return object_aclmask(TableSpaceRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3017		case OBJECT_FDW:
3018			return object_aclmask(ForeignDataWrapperRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3019		case OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER:
3020			return object_aclmask(ForeignServerRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3021		case OBJECT_EVENT_TRIGGER:
3022			elog(ERROR, "grantable rights not supported for event triggers");
3023			/* not reached, but keep compiler quiet */
3024			return ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
3025		case OBJECT_TYPE:
3026			return object_aclmask(TypeRelationId, object_oid, roleid, mask, how);
3027		default:
3028			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized object type: %d",
3029				 (int) objtype);
3030			/* not reached, but keep compiler quiet */
3031			return ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
3032	}
3033}
3034
3035
3036/* ****************************************************************
3037 * Exported routines for examining a user's privileges for various objects
3038 *
3039 * See aclmask() for a description of the common API for these functions.
3040 * ****************************************************************
3041 */
3042
3043/*
3044 * Generic routine for examining a user's privileges for an object
3045 */
3046static AclMode
3047object_aclmask(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid,
3048			   AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
3049{
3050	return object_aclmask_ext(classid, objectid, roleid, mask, how, NULL);
3051}
3052
3053/*
3054 * Generic routine for examining a user's privileges for an object,
3055 * with is_missing
3056 */
3057static AclMode
3058object_aclmask_ext(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid,
3059				   AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
3060				   bool *is_missing)
3061{
3062	int			cacheid;
3063	AclMode		result;
3064	HeapTuple	tuple;
3065	Datum		aclDatum;
3066	bool		isNull;
3067	Acl		   *acl;
3068	Oid			ownerId;
3069
3070	/* Special cases */
3071	switch (classid)
3072	{
3073		case NamespaceRelationId:
3074			return pg_namespace_aclmask_ext(objectid, roleid, mask, how,
3075											is_missing);
3076		case TypeRelationId:
3077			return pg_type_aclmask_ext(objectid, roleid, mask, how,
3078									   is_missing);
3079	}
3080
3081	/* Even more special cases */
3082	Assert(classid != RelationRelationId);	/* should use pg_class_acl* */
3083	Assert(classid != LargeObjectMetadataRelationId);	/* should use
3084														 * pg_largeobject_acl* */
3085
3086	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
3087	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3088		return mask;
3089
3090	/*
3091	 * Get the object's ACL from its catalog
3092	 */
3093
3094	cacheid = get_object_catcache_oid(classid);
3095
3096	tuple = SearchSysCache1(cacheid, ObjectIdGetDatum(objectid));
3097	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3098	{
3099		if (is_missing != NULL)
3100		{
3101			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3102			*is_missing = true;
3103			return 0;
3104		}
3105		else
3106			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for %s %u",
3107				 get_object_class_descr(classid), objectid);
3108	}
3109
3110	ownerId = DatumGetObjectId(SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(cacheid,
3111													  tuple,
3112													  get_object_attnum_owner(classid)));
3113
3114	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(cacheid, tuple, get_object_attnum_acl(classid),
3115							   &isNull);
3116	if (isNull)
3117	{
3118		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3119		acl = acldefault(get_object_type(classid, objectid), ownerId);
3120		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3121	}
3122	else
3123	{
3124		/* detoast ACL if necessary */
3125		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3126	}
3127
3128	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3129
3130	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3131	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3132		pfree(acl);
3133
3134	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3135
3136	return result;
3137}
3138
3139/*
3140 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a column
3141 *
3142 * Note: this considers only privileges granted specifically on the column.
3143 * It is caller's responsibility to take relation-level privileges into account
3144 * as appropriate.  (For the same reason, we have no special case for
3145 * superuser-ness here.)
3146 */
3147static AclMode
3148pg_attribute_aclmask(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum, Oid roleid,
3149					 AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
3150{
3151	return pg_attribute_aclmask_ext(table_oid, attnum, roleid,
3152									mask, how, NULL);
3153}
3154
3155/*
3156 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a column, with is_missing
3157 */
3158static AclMode
3159pg_attribute_aclmask_ext(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum, Oid roleid,
3160						 AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how, bool *is_missing)
3161{
3162	AclMode		result;
3163	HeapTuple	classTuple;
3164	HeapTuple	attTuple;
3165	Form_pg_class classForm;
3166	Form_pg_attribute attributeForm;
3167	Datum		aclDatum;
3168	bool		isNull;
3169	Acl		   *acl;
3170	Oid			ownerId;
3171
3172	/*
3173	 * First, get the column's ACL from its pg_attribute entry
3174	 */
3175	attTuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
3176							   ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid),
3177							   Int16GetDatum(attnum));
3178	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attTuple))
3179	{
3180		if (is_missing != NULL)
3181		{
3182			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3183			*is_missing = true;
3184			return 0;
3185		}
3186		else
3187			ereport(ERROR,
3188					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
3189					 errmsg("attribute %d of relation with OID %u does not exist",
3190							attnum, table_oid)));
3191	}
3192
3193	attributeForm = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(attTuple);
3194
3195	/* Check dropped columns, too */
3196	if (attributeForm->attisdropped)
3197	{
3198		if (is_missing != NULL)
3199		{
3200			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3201			*is_missing = true;
3202			ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
3203			return 0;
3204		}
3205		else
3206			ereport(ERROR,
3207					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
3208					 errmsg("attribute %d of relation with OID %u does not exist",
3209							attnum, table_oid)));
3210	}
3211
3212	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(ATTNUM, attTuple, Anum_pg_attribute_attacl,
3213							   &isNull);
3214
3215	/*
3216	 * Here we hard-wire knowledge that the default ACL for a column grants no
3217	 * privileges, so that we can fall out quickly in the very common case
3218	 * where attacl is null.
3219	 */
3220	if (isNull)
3221	{
3222		ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
3223		return 0;
3224	}
3225
3226	/*
3227	 * Must get the relation's ownerId from pg_class.  Since we already found
3228	 * a pg_attribute entry, the only likely reason for this to fail is that a
3229	 * concurrent DROP of the relation committed since then (which could only
3230	 * happen if we don't have lock on the relation).  Treat that similarly to
3231	 * not finding the attribute entry.
3232	 */
3233	classTuple = SearchSysCache1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid));
3234	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(classTuple))
3235	{
3236		ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
3237		if (is_missing != NULL)
3238		{
3239			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3240			*is_missing = true;
3241			return 0;
3242		}
3243		else
3244			ereport(ERROR,
3245					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
3246					 errmsg("relation with OID %u does not exist",
3247							table_oid)));
3248	}
3249	classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(classTuple);
3250
3251	ownerId = classForm->relowner;
3252
3253	ReleaseSysCache(classTuple);
3254
3255	/* detoast column's ACL if necessary */
3256	acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3257
3258	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3259
3260	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3261	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3262		pfree(acl);
3263
3264	ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
3265
3266	return result;
3267}
3268
3269/*
3270 * Exported routine for examining a user's privileges for a table
3271 */
3272AclMode
3273pg_class_aclmask(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid,
3274				 AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
3275{
3276	return pg_class_aclmask_ext(table_oid, roleid, mask, how, NULL);
3277}
3278
3279/*
3280 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a table, with is_missing
3281 */
3282static AclMode
3283pg_class_aclmask_ext(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mask,
3284					 AclMaskHow how, bool *is_missing)
3285{
3286	AclMode		result;
3287	HeapTuple	tuple;
3288	Form_pg_class classForm;
3289	Datum		aclDatum;
3290	bool		isNull;
3291	Acl		   *acl;
3292	Oid			ownerId;
3293
3294	/*
3295	 * Must get the relation's tuple from pg_class
3296	 */
3297	tuple = SearchSysCache1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid));
3298	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3299	{
3300		if (is_missing != NULL)
3301		{
3302			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3303			*is_missing = true;
3304			return 0;
3305		}
3306		else
3307			ereport(ERROR,
3308					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
3309					 errmsg("relation with OID %u does not exist",
3310							table_oid)));
3311	}
3312
3313	classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3314
3315	/*
3316	 * Deny anyone permission to update a system catalog unless
3317	 * pg_authid.rolsuper is set.
3318	 *
3319	 * As of 7.4 we have some updatable system views; those shouldn't be
3320	 * protected in this way.  Assume the view rules can take care of
3321	 * themselves.  ACL_USAGE is if we ever have system sequences.
3322	 */
3323	if ((mask & (ACL_INSERT | ACL_UPDATE | ACL_DELETE | ACL_TRUNCATE | ACL_USAGE)) &&
3324		IsSystemClass(table_oid, classForm) &&
3325		classForm->relkind != RELKIND_VIEW &&
3326		!superuser_arg(roleid))
3327		mask &= ~(ACL_INSERT | ACL_UPDATE | ACL_DELETE | ACL_TRUNCATE | ACL_USAGE);
3328
3329	/*
3330	 * Otherwise, superusers bypass all permission-checking.
3331	 */
3332	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3333	{
3334		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3335		return mask;
3336	}
3337
3338	/*
3339	 * Normal case: get the relation's ACL from pg_class
3340	 */
3341	ownerId = classForm->relowner;
3342
3343	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(RELOID, tuple, Anum_pg_class_relacl,
3344							   &isNull);
3345	if (isNull)
3346	{
3347		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3348		switch (classForm->relkind)
3349		{
3350			case RELKIND_SEQUENCE:
3351				acl = acldefault(OBJECT_SEQUENCE, ownerId);
3352				break;
3353			default:
3354				acl = acldefault(OBJECT_TABLE, ownerId);
3355				break;
3356		}
3357		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3358	}
3359	else
3360	{
3361		/* detoast rel's ACL if necessary */
3362		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3363	}
3364
3365	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3366
3367	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3368	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3369		pfree(acl);
3370
3371	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3372
3373	/*
3374	 * Check if ACL_SELECT is being checked and, if so, and not set already as
3375	 * part of the result, then check if the user is a member of the
3376	 * pg_read_all_data role, which allows read access to all relations.
3377	 */
3378	if (mask & ACL_SELECT && !(result & ACL_SELECT) &&
3379		has_privs_of_role(roleid, ROLE_PG_READ_ALL_DATA))
3380		result |= ACL_SELECT;
3381
3382	/*
3383	 * Check if ACL_INSERT, ACL_UPDATE, or ACL_DELETE is being checked and, if
3384	 * so, and not set already as part of the result, then check if the user
3385	 * is a member of the pg_write_all_data role, which allows
3386	 * INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE access to all relations (except system catalogs,
3387	 * which requires superuser, see above).
3388	 */
3389	if (mask & (ACL_INSERT | ACL_UPDATE | ACL_DELETE) &&
3390		!(result & (ACL_INSERT | ACL_UPDATE | ACL_DELETE)) &&
3391		has_privs_of_role(roleid, ROLE_PG_WRITE_ALL_DATA))
3392		result |= (mask & (ACL_INSERT | ACL_UPDATE | ACL_DELETE));
3393
3394	/*
3395	 * Check if ACL_MAINTAIN is being checked and, if so, and not already set
3396	 * as part of the result, then check if the user is a member of the
3397	 * pg_maintain role, which allows VACUUM, ANALYZE, CLUSTER, REFRESH
3398	 * MATERIALIZED VIEW, REINDEX, and LOCK TABLE on all relations.
3399	 */
3400	if (mask & ACL_MAINTAIN &&
3401		!(result & ACL_MAINTAIN) &&
3402		has_privs_of_role(roleid, ROLE_PG_MAINTAIN))
3403		result |= ACL_MAINTAIN;
3404
3405	return result;
3406}
3407
3408/*
3409 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a configuration
3410 * parameter (GUC), identified by GUC name.
3411 */
3412static AclMode
3413pg_parameter_aclmask(const char *name, Oid roleid, AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
3414{
3415	AclMode		result;
3416	char	   *parname;
3417	text	   *partext;
3418	HeapTuple	tuple;
3419
3420	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
3421	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3422		return mask;
3423
3424	/* Convert name to the form it should have in pg_parameter_acl... */
3425	parname = convert_GUC_name_for_parameter_acl(name);
3426	partext = cstring_to_text(parname);
3427
3428	/* ... and look it up */
3429	tuple = SearchSysCache1(PARAMETERACLNAME, PointerGetDatum(partext));
3430
3431	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3432	{
3433		/* If no entry, GUC has no permissions for non-superusers */
3434		result = ACL_NO_RIGHTS;
3435	}
3436	else
3437	{
3438		Datum		aclDatum;
3439		bool		isNull;
3440		Acl		   *acl;
3441
3442		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(PARAMETERACLNAME, tuple,
3443								   Anum_pg_parameter_acl_paracl,
3444								   &isNull);
3445		if (isNull)
3446		{
3447			/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3448			acl = acldefault(OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID);
3449			aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3450		}
3451		else
3452		{
3453			/* detoast ACL if necessary */
3454			acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3455		}
3456
3457		result = aclmask(acl, roleid, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID, mask, how);
3458
3459		/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3460		if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3461			pfree(acl);
3462
3463		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3464	}
3465
3466	pfree(parname);
3467	pfree(partext);
3468
3469	return result;
3470}
3471
3472/*
3473 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a configuration
3474 * parameter (GUC), identified by the OID of its pg_parameter_acl entry.
3475 */
3476static AclMode
3477pg_parameter_acl_aclmask(Oid acl_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how)
3478{
3479	AclMode		result;
3480	HeapTuple	tuple;
3481	Datum		aclDatum;
3482	bool		isNull;
3483	Acl		   *acl;
3484
3485	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
3486	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3487		return mask;
3488
3489	/* Get the ACL from pg_parameter_acl */
3490	tuple = SearchSysCache1(PARAMETERACLOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(acl_oid));
3491	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3492		ereport(ERROR,
3493				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3494				 errmsg("parameter ACL with OID %u does not exist",
3495						acl_oid)));
3496
3497	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(PARAMETERACLOID, tuple,
3498							   Anum_pg_parameter_acl_paracl,
3499							   &isNull);
3500	if (isNull)
3501	{
3502		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3503		acl = acldefault(OBJECT_PARAMETER_ACL, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID);
3504		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3505	}
3506	else
3507	{
3508		/* detoast ACL if necessary */
3509		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3510	}
3511
3512	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID, mask, how);
3513
3514	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3515	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3516		pfree(acl);
3517
3518	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3519
3520	return result;
3521}
3522
3523/*
3524 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a largeobject
3525 *
3526 * When a large object is opened for reading, it is opened relative to the
3527 * caller's snapshot, but when it is opened for writing, a current
3528 * MVCC snapshot will be used.  See doc/src/sgml/lobj.sgml.  This function
3529 * takes a snapshot argument so that the permissions check can be made
3530 * relative to the same snapshot that will be used to read the underlying
3531 * data.  The caller will actually pass NULL for an instantaneous MVCC
3532 * snapshot, since all we do with the snapshot argument is pass it through
3533 * to systable_beginscan().
3534 */
3535static AclMode
3536pg_largeobject_aclmask_snapshot(Oid lobj_oid, Oid roleid,
3537								AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
3538								Snapshot snapshot)
3539{
3540	AclMode		result;
3541	Relation	pg_lo_meta;
3542	ScanKeyData entry[1];
3543	SysScanDesc scan;
3544	HeapTuple	tuple;
3545	Datum		aclDatum;
3546	bool		isNull;
3547	Acl		   *acl;
3548	Oid			ownerId;
3549
3550	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
3551	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3552		return mask;
3553
3554	/*
3555	 * Get the largeobject's ACL from pg_largeobject_metadata
3556	 */
3557	pg_lo_meta = table_open(LargeObjectMetadataRelationId,
3558							AccessShareLock);
3559
3560	ScanKeyInit(&entry[0],
3561				Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_oid,
3562				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3563				ObjectIdGetDatum(lobj_oid));
3564
3565	scan = systable_beginscan(pg_lo_meta,
3566							  LargeObjectMetadataOidIndexId, true,
3567							  snapshot, 1, entry);
3568
3569	tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
3570	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3571		ereport(ERROR,
3572				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3573				 errmsg("large object %u does not exist", lobj_oid)));
3574
3575	ownerId = ((Form_pg_largeobject_metadata) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->lomowner;
3576
3577	aclDatum = heap_getattr(tuple, Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_lomacl,
3578							RelationGetDescr(pg_lo_meta), &isNull);
3579
3580	if (isNull)
3581	{
3582		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3583		acl = acldefault(OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT, ownerId);
3584		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3585	}
3586	else
3587	{
3588		/* detoast ACL if necessary */
3589		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3590	}
3591
3592	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3593
3594	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3595	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3596		pfree(acl);
3597
3598	systable_endscan(scan);
3599
3600	table_close(pg_lo_meta, AccessShareLock);
3601
3602	return result;
3603}
3604
3605/*
3606 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a namespace, with is_missing
3607 */
3608static AclMode
3609pg_namespace_aclmask_ext(Oid nsp_oid, Oid roleid,
3610						 AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
3611						 bool *is_missing)
3612{
3613	AclMode		result;
3614	HeapTuple	tuple;
3615	Datum		aclDatum;
3616	bool		isNull;
3617	Acl		   *acl;
3618	Oid			ownerId;
3619
3620	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
3621	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3622		return mask;
3623
3624	/*
3625	 * If we have been assigned this namespace as a temp namespace, check to
3626	 * make sure we have CREATE TEMP permission on the database, and if so act
3627	 * as though we have all standard (but not GRANT OPTION) permissions on
3628	 * the namespace.  If we don't have CREATE TEMP, act as though we have
3629	 * only USAGE (and not CREATE) rights.
3630	 *
3631	 * This may seem redundant given the check in InitTempTableNamespace, but
3632	 * it really isn't since current user ID may have changed since then. The
3633	 * upshot of this behavior is that a SECURITY DEFINER function can create
3634	 * temp tables that can then be accessed (if permission is granted) by
3635	 * code in the same session that doesn't have permissions to create temp
3636	 * tables.
3637	 *
3638	 * XXX Would it be safe to ereport a special error message as
3639	 * InitTempTableNamespace does?  Returning zero here means we'll get a
3640	 * generic "permission denied for schema pg_temp_N" message, which is not
3641	 * remarkably user-friendly.
3642	 */
3643	if (isTempNamespace(nsp_oid))
3644	{
3645		if (object_aclcheck_ext(DatabaseRelationId, MyDatabaseId, roleid,
3646								ACL_CREATE_TEMP, is_missing) == ACLCHECK_OK)
3647			return mask & ACL_ALL_RIGHTS_SCHEMA;
3648		else
3649			return mask & ACL_USAGE;
3650	}
3651
3652	/*
3653	 * Get the schema's ACL from pg_namespace
3654	 */
3655	tuple = SearchSysCache1(NAMESPACEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(nsp_oid));
3656	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3657	{
3658		if (is_missing != NULL)
3659		{
3660			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3661			*is_missing = true;
3662			return 0;
3663		}
3664		else
3665			ereport(ERROR,
3666					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_SCHEMA),
3667					 errmsg("schema with OID %u does not exist", nsp_oid)));
3668	}
3669
3670	ownerId = ((Form_pg_namespace) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->nspowner;
3671
3672	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(NAMESPACEOID, tuple, Anum_pg_namespace_nspacl,
3673							   &isNull);
3674	if (isNull)
3675	{
3676		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3677		acl = acldefault(OBJECT_SCHEMA, ownerId);
3678		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3679	}
3680	else
3681	{
3682		/* detoast ACL if necessary */
3683		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3684	}
3685
3686	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3687
3688	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3689	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3690		pfree(acl);
3691
3692	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3693
3694	/*
3695	 * Check if ACL_USAGE is being checked and, if so, and not set already as
3696	 * part of the result, then check if the user is a member of the
3697	 * pg_read_all_data or pg_write_all_data roles, which allow usage access
3698	 * to all schemas.
3699	 */
3700	if (mask & ACL_USAGE && !(result & ACL_USAGE) &&
3701		(has_privs_of_role(roleid, ROLE_PG_READ_ALL_DATA) ||
3702		 has_privs_of_role(roleid, ROLE_PG_WRITE_ALL_DATA)))
3703		result |= ACL_USAGE;
3704	return result;
3705}
3706
3707/*
3708 * Routine for examining a user's privileges for a type, with is_missing
3709 */
3710static AclMode
3711pg_type_aclmask_ext(Oid type_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mask, AclMaskHow how,
3712					bool *is_missing)
3713{
3714	AclMode		result;
3715	HeapTuple	tuple;
3716	Form_pg_type typeForm;
3717	Datum		aclDatum;
3718	bool		isNull;
3719	Acl		   *acl;
3720	Oid			ownerId;
3721
3722	/* Bypass permission checks for superusers */
3723	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
3724		return mask;
3725
3726	/*
3727	 * Must get the type's tuple from pg_type
3728	 */
3729	tuple = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(type_oid));
3730	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3731	{
3732		if (is_missing != NULL)
3733		{
3734			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3735			*is_missing = true;
3736			return 0;
3737		}
3738		else
3739			ereport(ERROR,
3740					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3741					 errmsg("type with OID %u does not exist",
3742							type_oid)));
3743	}
3744	typeForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3745
3746	/*
3747	 * "True" array types don't manage permissions of their own; consult the
3748	 * element type instead.
3749	 */
3750	if (IsTrueArrayType(typeForm))
3751	{
3752		Oid			elttype_oid = typeForm->typelem;
3753
3754		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3755
3756		tuple = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(elttype_oid));
3757		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3758		{
3759			if (is_missing != NULL)
3760			{
3761				/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3762				*is_missing = true;
3763				return 0;
3764			}
3765			else
3766				ereport(ERROR,
3767						(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3768						 errmsg("type with OID %u does not exist",
3769								elttype_oid)));
3770		}
3771		typeForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3772	}
3773
3774	/*
3775	 * Likewise, multirange types don't manage their own permissions; consult
3776	 * the associated range type.  (Note we must do this after the array step
3777	 * to get the right answer for arrays of multiranges.)
3778	 */
3779	if (typeForm->typtype == TYPTYPE_MULTIRANGE)
3780	{
3781		Oid			rangetype = get_multirange_range(typeForm->oid);
3782
3783		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3784
3785		tuple = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(rangetype));
3786		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3787		{
3788			if (is_missing != NULL)
3789			{
3790				/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3791				*is_missing = true;
3792				return 0;
3793			}
3794			else
3795				ereport(ERROR,
3796						(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3797						 errmsg("type with OID %u does not exist",
3798								rangetype)));
3799		}
3800		typeForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3801	}
3802
3803	/*
3804	 * Now get the type's owner and ACL from the tuple
3805	 */
3806	ownerId = typeForm->typowner;
3807
3808	aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(TYPEOID, tuple,
3809							   Anum_pg_type_typacl, &isNull);
3810	if (isNull)
3811	{
3812		/* No ACL, so build default ACL */
3813		acl = acldefault(OBJECT_TYPE, ownerId);
3814		aclDatum = (Datum) 0;
3815	}
3816	else
3817	{
3818		/* detoast rel's ACL if necessary */
3819		acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
3820	}
3821
3822	result = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mask, how);
3823
3824	/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
3825	if (acl && (Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
3826		pfree(acl);
3827
3828	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
3829
3830	return result;
3831}
3832
3833/*
3834 * Exported generic routine for checking a user's access privileges to an object
3835 */
3836AclResult
3837object_aclcheck(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid, AclMode mode)
3838{
3839	return object_aclcheck_ext(classid, objectid, roleid, mode, NULL);
3840}
3841
3842/*
3843 * Exported generic routine for checking a user's access privileges to an
3844 * object, with is_missing
3845 */
3846AclResult
3847object_aclcheck_ext(Oid classid, Oid objectid,
3848					Oid roleid, AclMode mode,
3849					bool *is_missing)
3850{
3851	if (object_aclmask_ext(classid, objectid, roleid, mode, ACLMASK_ANY,
3852						   is_missing) != 0)
3853		return ACLCHECK_OK;
3854	else
3855		return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
3856}
3857
3858/*
3859 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a column
3860 *
3861 * Returns ACLCHECK_OK if the user has any of the privileges identified by
3862 * 'mode'; otherwise returns a suitable error code (in practice, always
3863 * ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV).
3864 *
3865 * As with pg_attribute_aclmask, only privileges granted directly on the
3866 * column are considered here.
3867 */
3868AclResult
3869pg_attribute_aclcheck(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum,
3870					  Oid roleid, AclMode mode)
3871{
3872	return pg_attribute_aclcheck_ext(table_oid, attnum, roleid, mode, NULL);
3873}
3874
3875
3876/*
3877 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a column,
3878 * with is_missing
3879 */
3880AclResult
3881pg_attribute_aclcheck_ext(Oid table_oid, AttrNumber attnum,
3882						  Oid roleid, AclMode mode, bool *is_missing)
3883{
3884	if (pg_attribute_aclmask_ext(table_oid, attnum, roleid, mode,
3885								 ACLMASK_ANY, is_missing) != 0)
3886		return ACLCHECK_OK;
3887	else
3888		return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
3889}
3890
3891/*
3892 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to any/all columns
3893 *
3894 * If 'how' is ACLMASK_ANY, then returns ACLCHECK_OK if user has any of the
3895 * privileges identified by 'mode' on any non-dropped column in the relation;
3896 * otherwise returns a suitable error code (in practice, always
3897 * ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV).
3898 *
3899 * If 'how' is ACLMASK_ALL, then returns ACLCHECK_OK if user has any of the
3900 * privileges identified by 'mode' on each non-dropped column in the relation
3901 * (and there must be at least one such column); otherwise returns a suitable
3902 * error code (in practice, always ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV).
3903 *
3904 * As with pg_attribute_aclmask, only privileges granted directly on the
3905 * column(s) are considered here.
3906 *
3907 * Note: system columns are not considered here; there are cases where that
3908 * might be appropriate but there are also cases where it wouldn't.
3909 */
3910AclResult
3911pg_attribute_aclcheck_all(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mode,
3912						  AclMaskHow how)
3913{
3914	return pg_attribute_aclcheck_all_ext(table_oid, roleid, mode, how, NULL);
3915}
3916
3917/*
3918 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to any/all columns,
3919 * with is_missing
3920 */
3921AclResult
3922pg_attribute_aclcheck_all_ext(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid,
3923							  AclMode mode, AclMaskHow how,
3924							  bool *is_missing)
3925{
3926	AclResult	result;
3927	HeapTuple	classTuple;
3928	Form_pg_class classForm;
3929	Oid			ownerId;
3930	AttrNumber	nattrs;
3931	AttrNumber	curr_att;
3932
3933	/*
3934	 * Must fetch pg_class row to get owner ID and number of attributes.
3935	 */
3936	classTuple = SearchSysCache1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid));
3937	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(classTuple))
3938	{
3939		if (is_missing != NULL)
3940		{
3941			/* return "no privileges" instead of throwing an error */
3942			*is_missing = true;
3943			return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
3944		}
3945		else
3946			ereport(ERROR,
3947					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
3948					 errmsg("relation with OID %u does not exist",
3949							table_oid)));
3950	}
3951	classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(classTuple);
3952
3953	ownerId = classForm->relowner;
3954	nattrs = classForm->relnatts;
3955
3956	ReleaseSysCache(classTuple);
3957
3958	/*
3959	 * Initialize result in case there are no non-dropped columns.  We want to
3960	 * report failure in such cases for either value of 'how'.
3961	 */
3962	result = ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
3963
3964	for (curr_att = 1; curr_att <= nattrs; curr_att++)
3965	{
3966		HeapTuple	attTuple;
3967		Datum		aclDatum;
3968		bool		isNull;
3969		Acl		   *acl;
3970		AclMode		attmask;
3971
3972		attTuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
3973								   ObjectIdGetDatum(table_oid),
3974								   Int16GetDatum(curr_att));
3975
3976		/*
3977		 * Lookup failure probably indicates that the table was just dropped,
3978		 * but we'll treat it the same as a dropped column rather than
3979		 * throwing error.
3980		 */
3981		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attTuple))
3982			continue;
3983
3984		/* ignore dropped columns */
3985		if (((Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(attTuple))->attisdropped)
3986		{
3987			ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
3988			continue;
3989		}
3990
3991		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(ATTNUM, attTuple, Anum_pg_attribute_attacl,
3992								   &isNull);
3993
3994		/*
3995		 * Here we hard-wire knowledge that the default ACL for a column
3996		 * grants no privileges, so that we can fall out quickly in the very
3997		 * common case where attacl is null.
3998		 */
3999		if (isNull)
4000			attmask = 0;
4001		else
4002		{
4003			/* detoast column's ACL if necessary */
4004			acl = DatumGetAclP(aclDatum);
4005
4006			attmask = aclmask(acl, roleid, ownerId, mode, ACLMASK_ANY);
4007
4008			/* if we have a detoasted copy, free it */
4009			if ((Pointer) acl != DatumGetPointer(aclDatum))
4010				pfree(acl);
4011		}
4012
4013		ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
4014
4015		if (attmask != 0)
4016		{
4017			result = ACLCHECK_OK;
4018			if (how == ACLMASK_ANY)
4019				break;			/* succeed on any success */
4020		}
4021		else
4022		{
4023			result = ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
4024			if (how == ACLMASK_ALL)
4025				break;			/* fail on any failure */
4026		}
4027	}
4028
4029	return result;
4030}
4031
4032/*
4033 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a table
4034 *
4035 * Returns ACLCHECK_OK if the user has any of the privileges identified by
4036 * 'mode'; otherwise returns a suitable error code (in practice, always
4037 * ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV).
4038 */
4039AclResult
4040pg_class_aclcheck(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mode)
4041{
4042	return pg_class_aclcheck_ext(table_oid, roleid, mode, NULL);
4043}
4044
4045/*
4046 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a table,
4047 * with is_missing
4048 */
4049AclResult
4050pg_class_aclcheck_ext(Oid table_oid, Oid roleid,
4051					  AclMode mode, bool *is_missing)
4052{
4053	if (pg_class_aclmask_ext(table_oid, roleid, mode,
4054							 ACLMASK_ANY, is_missing) != 0)
4055		return ACLCHECK_OK;
4056	else
4057		return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
4058}
4059
4060/*
4061 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a configuration
4062 * parameter (GUC), identified by GUC name.
4063 */
4064AclResult
4065pg_parameter_aclcheck(const char *name, Oid roleid, AclMode mode)
4066{
4067	if (pg_parameter_aclmask(name, roleid, mode, ACLMASK_ANY) != 0)
4068		return ACLCHECK_OK;
4069	else
4070		return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
4071}
4072
4073/*
4074 * Exported routine for checking a user's access privileges to a largeobject
4075 */
4076AclResult
4077pg_largeobject_aclcheck_snapshot(Oid lobj_oid, Oid roleid, AclMode mode,
4078								 Snapshot snapshot)
4079{
4080	if (pg_largeobject_aclmask_snapshot(lobj_oid, roleid, mode,
4081										ACLMASK_ANY, snapshot) != 0)
4082		return ACLCHECK_OK;
4083	else
4084		return ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV;
4085}
4086
4087/*
4088 * Generic ownership check for an object
4089 */
4090bool
4091object_ownercheck(Oid classid, Oid objectid, Oid roleid)
4092{
4093	int			cacheid;
4094	Oid			ownerId;
4095
4096	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
4097	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
4098		return true;
4099
4100	/* For large objects, the catalog to consult is pg_largeobject_metadata */
4101	if (classid == LargeObjectRelationId)
4102		classid = LargeObjectMetadataRelationId;
4103
4104	cacheid = get_object_catcache_oid(classid);
4105	if (cacheid != -1)
4106	{
4107		/* we can get the object's tuple from the syscache */
4108		HeapTuple	tuple;
4109
4110		tuple = SearchSysCache1(cacheid, ObjectIdGetDatum(objectid));
4111		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4112			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for %s %u",
4113				 get_object_class_descr(classid), objectid);
4114
4115		ownerId = DatumGetObjectId(SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(cacheid,
4116														  tuple,
4117														  get_object_attnum_owner(classid)));
4118		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4119	}
4120	else
4121	{
4122		/* for catalogs without an appropriate syscache */
4123		Relation	rel;
4124		ScanKeyData entry[1];
4125		SysScanDesc scan;
4126		HeapTuple	tuple;
4127		bool		isnull;
4128
4129		rel = table_open(classid, AccessShareLock);
4130
4131		ScanKeyInit(&entry[0],
4132					get_object_attnum_oid(classid),
4133					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4134					ObjectIdGetDatum(objectid));
4135
4136		scan = systable_beginscan(rel,
4137								  get_object_oid_index(classid), true,
4138								  NULL, 1, entry);
4139
4140		tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
4141		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4142			elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for %s %u",
4143				 get_object_class_descr(classid), objectid);
4144
4145		ownerId = DatumGetObjectId(heap_getattr(tuple,
4146												get_object_attnum_owner(classid),
4147												RelationGetDescr(rel),
4148												&isnull));
4149		Assert(!isnull);
4150
4151		systable_endscan(scan);
4152		table_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
4153	}
4154
4155	return has_privs_of_role(roleid, ownerId);
4156}
4157
4158/*
4159 * Check whether specified role has CREATEROLE privilege (or is a superuser)
4160 *
4161 * Note: roles do not have owners per se; instead we use this test in
4162 * places where an ownership-like permissions test is needed for a role.
4163 * Be sure to apply it to the role trying to do the operation, not the
4164 * role being operated on!	Also note that this generally should not be
4165 * considered enough privilege if the target role is a superuser.
4166 * (We don't handle that consideration here because we want to give a
4167 * separate error message for such cases, so the caller has to deal with it.)
4168 */
4169bool
4170has_createrole_privilege(Oid roleid)
4171{
4172	bool		result = false;
4173	HeapTuple	utup;
4174
4175	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
4176	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
4177		return true;
4178
4179	utup = SearchSysCache1(AUTHOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(roleid));
4180	if (HeapTupleIsValid(utup))
4181	{
4182		result = ((Form_pg_authid) GETSTRUCT(utup))->rolcreaterole;
4183		ReleaseSysCache(utup);
4184	}
4185	return result;
4186}
4187
4188bool
4189has_bypassrls_privilege(Oid roleid)
4190{
4191	bool		result = false;
4192	HeapTuple	utup;
4193
4194	/* Superusers bypass all permission checking. */
4195	if (superuser_arg(roleid))
4196		return true;
4197
4198	utup = SearchSysCache1(AUTHOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(roleid));
4199	if (HeapTupleIsValid(utup))
4200	{
4201		result = ((Form_pg_authid) GETSTRUCT(utup))->rolbypassrls;
4202		ReleaseSysCache(utup);
4203	}
4204	return result;
4205}
4206
4207/*
4208 * Fetch pg_default_acl entry for given role, namespace and object type
4209 * (object type must be given in pg_default_acl's encoding).
4210 * Returns NULL if no such entry.
4211 */
4212static Acl *
4213get_default_acl_internal(Oid roleId, Oid nsp_oid, char objtype)
4214{
4215	Acl		   *result = NULL;
4216	HeapTuple	tuple;
4217
4218	tuple = SearchSysCache3(DEFACLROLENSPOBJ,
4219							ObjectIdGetDatum(roleId),
4220							ObjectIdGetDatum(nsp_oid),
4221							CharGetDatum(objtype));
4222
4223	if (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4224	{
4225		Datum		aclDatum;
4226		bool		isNull;
4227
4228		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(DEFACLROLENSPOBJ, tuple,
4229								   Anum_pg_default_acl_defaclacl,
4230								   &isNull);
4231		if (!isNull)
4232			result = DatumGetAclPCopy(aclDatum);
4233		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4234	}
4235
4236	return result;
4237}
4238
4239/*
4240 * Get default permissions for newly created object within given schema
4241 *
4242 * Returns NULL if built-in system defaults should be used.
4243 *
4244 * If the result is not NULL, caller must call recordDependencyOnNewAcl
4245 * once the OID of the new object is known.
4246 */
4247Acl *
4248get_user_default_acl(ObjectType objtype, Oid ownerId, Oid nsp_oid)
4249{
4250	Acl		   *result;
4251	Acl		   *glob_acl;
4252	Acl		   *schema_acl;
4253	Acl		   *def_acl;
4254	char		defaclobjtype;
4255
4256	/*
4257	 * Use NULL during bootstrap, since pg_default_acl probably isn't there
4258	 * yet.
4259	 */
4260	if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
4261		return NULL;
4262
4263	/* Check if object type is supported in pg_default_acl */
4264	switch (objtype)
4265	{
4266		case OBJECT_TABLE:
4267			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_RELATION;
4268			break;
4269
4270		case OBJECT_SEQUENCE:
4271			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_SEQUENCE;
4272			break;
4273
4274		case OBJECT_FUNCTION:
4275			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_FUNCTION;
4276			break;
4277
4278		case OBJECT_TYPE:
4279			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_TYPE;
4280			break;
4281
4282		case OBJECT_SCHEMA:
4283			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_NAMESPACE;
4284			break;
4285
4286		case OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT:
4287			defaclobjtype = DEFACLOBJ_LARGEOBJECT;
4288			break;
4289
4290		default:
4291			return NULL;
4292	}
4293
4294	/* Look up the relevant pg_default_acl entries */
4295	glob_acl = get_default_acl_internal(ownerId, InvalidOid, defaclobjtype);
4296	schema_acl = get_default_acl_internal(ownerId, nsp_oid, defaclobjtype);
4297
4298	/* Quick out if neither entry exists */
4299	if (glob_acl == NULL && schema_acl == NULL)
4300		return NULL;
4301
4302	/* We need to know the hard-wired default value, too */
4303	def_acl = acldefault(objtype, ownerId);
4304
4305	/* If there's no global entry, substitute the hard-wired default */
4306	if (glob_acl == NULL)
4307		glob_acl = def_acl;
4308
4309	/* Merge in any per-schema privileges */
4310	result = aclmerge(glob_acl, schema_acl, ownerId);
4311
4312	/*
4313	 * For efficiency, we want to return NULL if the result equals default.
4314	 * This requires sorting both arrays to get an accurate comparison.
4315	 */
4316	aclitemsort(result);
4317	aclitemsort(def_acl);
4318	if (aclequal(result, def_acl))
4319		result = NULL;
4320
4321	return result;
4322}
4323
4324/*
4325 * Record dependencies on roles mentioned in a new object's ACL.
4326 */
4327void
4328recordDependencyOnNewAcl(Oid classId, Oid objectId, int32 objsubId,
4329						 Oid ownerId, Acl *acl)
4330{
4331	int			nmembers;
4332	Oid		   *members;
4333
4334	/* Nothing to do if ACL is defaulted */
4335	if (acl == NULL)
4336		return;
4337
4338	/* Extract roles mentioned in ACL */
4339	nmembers = aclmembers(acl, &members);
4340
4341	/* Update the shared dependency ACL info */
4342	updateAclDependencies(classId, objectId, objsubId,
4343						  ownerId,
4344						  0, NULL,
4345						  nmembers, members);
4346}
4347
4348/*
4349 * Record initial privileges for the top-level object passed in.
4350 *
4351 * For the object passed in, this will record its ACL (if any) and the ACLs of
4352 * any sub-objects (eg: columns) into pg_init_privs.
4353 */
4354void
4355recordExtObjInitPriv(Oid objoid, Oid classoid)
4356{
4357	/*
4358	 * pg_class / pg_attribute
4359	 *
4360	 * If this is a relation then we need to see if there are any sub-objects
4361	 * (eg: columns) for it and, if so, be sure to call
4362	 * recordExtensionInitPrivWorker() for each one.
4363	 */
4364	if (classoid == RelationRelationId)
4365	{
4366		Form_pg_class pg_class_tuple;
4367		Datum		aclDatum;
4368		bool		isNull;
4369		HeapTuple	tuple;
4370
4371		tuple = SearchSysCache1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid));
4372		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4373			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u", objoid);
4374		pg_class_tuple = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
4375
4376		/*
4377		 * Indexes don't have permissions, neither do the pg_class rows for
4378		 * composite types.  (These cases are unreachable given the
4379		 * restrictions in ALTER EXTENSION ADD, but let's check anyway.)
4380		 */
4381		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX ||
4382			pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX ||
4383			pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
4384		{
4385			ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4386			return;
4387		}
4388
4389		/*
4390		 * If this isn't a sequence then it's possibly going to have
4391		 * column-level ACLs associated with it.
4392		 */
4393		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind != RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
4394		{
4395			AttrNumber	curr_att;
4396			AttrNumber	nattrs = pg_class_tuple->relnatts;
4397
4398			for (curr_att = 1; curr_att <= nattrs; curr_att++)
4399			{
4400				HeapTuple	attTuple;
4401				Datum		attaclDatum;
4402
4403				attTuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
4404										   ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid),
4405										   Int16GetDatum(curr_att));
4406
4407				if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attTuple))
4408					continue;
4409
4410				/* ignore dropped columns */
4411				if (((Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(attTuple))->attisdropped)
4412				{
4413					ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
4414					continue;
4415				}
4416
4417				attaclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(ATTNUM, attTuple,
4418											  Anum_pg_attribute_attacl,
4419											  &isNull);
4420
4421				/* no need to do anything for a NULL ACL */
4422				if (isNull)
4423				{
4424					ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
4425					continue;
4426				}
4427
4428				recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, curr_att,
4429											  DatumGetAclP(attaclDatum));
4430
4431				ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
4432			}
4433		}
4434
4435		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(RELOID, tuple, Anum_pg_class_relacl,
4436								   &isNull);
4437
4438		/* Add the record, if any, for the top-level object */
4439		if (!isNull)
4440			recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, 0,
4441										  DatumGetAclP(aclDatum));
4442
4443		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4444	}
4445	else if (classoid == LargeObjectRelationId)
4446	{
4447		/* For large objects, we must consult pg_largeobject_metadata */
4448		Datum		aclDatum;
4449		bool		isNull;
4450		HeapTuple	tuple;
4451		ScanKeyData entry[1];
4452		SysScanDesc scan;
4453		Relation	relation;
4454
4455		/*
4456		 * Note: this is dead code, given that we don't allow large objects to
4457		 * be made extension members.  But it seems worth carrying in case
4458		 * some future caller of this function has need for it.
4459		 */
4460		relation = table_open(LargeObjectMetadataRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4461
4462		/* There's no syscache for pg_largeobject_metadata */
4463		ScanKeyInit(&entry[0],
4464					Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_oid,
4465					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4466					ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid));
4467
4468		scan = systable_beginscan(relation,
4469								  LargeObjectMetadataOidIndexId, true,
4470								  NULL, 1, entry);
4471
4472		tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
4473		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4474			elog(ERROR, "could not find tuple for large object %u", objoid);
4475
4476		aclDatum = heap_getattr(tuple,
4477								Anum_pg_largeobject_metadata_lomacl,
4478								RelationGetDescr(relation), &isNull);
4479
4480		/* Add the record, if any, for the top-level object */
4481		if (!isNull)
4482			recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, 0,
4483										  DatumGetAclP(aclDatum));
4484
4485		systable_endscan(scan);
4486	}
4487	/* This will error on unsupported classoid. */
4488	else if (get_object_attnum_acl(classoid) != InvalidAttrNumber)
4489	{
4490		int			cacheid;
4491		Datum		aclDatum;
4492		bool		isNull;
4493		HeapTuple	tuple;
4494
4495		cacheid = get_object_catcache_oid(classoid);
4496		tuple = SearchSysCache1(cacheid, ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid));
4497		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4498			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for %s %u",
4499				 get_object_class_descr(classoid), objoid);
4500
4501		aclDatum = SysCacheGetAttr(cacheid, tuple,
4502								   get_object_attnum_acl(classoid),
4503								   &isNull);
4504
4505		/* Add the record, if any, for the top-level object */
4506		if (!isNull)
4507			recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, 0,
4508										  DatumGetAclP(aclDatum));
4509
4510		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4511	}
4512}
4513
4514/*
4515 * For the object passed in, remove its ACL and the ACLs of any object subIds
4516 * from pg_init_privs (via recordExtensionInitPrivWorker()).
4517 */
4518void
4519removeExtObjInitPriv(Oid objoid, Oid classoid)
4520{
4521	/*
4522	 * If this is a relation then we need to see if there are any sub-objects
4523	 * (eg: columns) for it and, if so, be sure to call
4524	 * recordExtensionInitPrivWorker() for each one.
4525	 */
4526	if (classoid == RelationRelationId)
4527	{
4528		Form_pg_class pg_class_tuple;
4529		HeapTuple	tuple;
4530
4531		tuple = SearchSysCache1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid));
4532		if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4533			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u", objoid);
4534		pg_class_tuple = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
4535
4536		/*
4537		 * Indexes don't have permissions, neither do the pg_class rows for
4538		 * composite types.  (These cases are unreachable given the
4539		 * restrictions in ALTER EXTENSION DROP, but let's check anyway.)
4540		 */
4541		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX ||
4542			pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX ||
4543			pg_class_tuple->relkind == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
4544		{
4545			ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4546			return;
4547		}
4548
4549		/*
4550		 * If this isn't a sequence then it's possibly going to have
4551		 * column-level ACLs associated with it.
4552		 */
4553		if (pg_class_tuple->relkind != RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
4554		{
4555			AttrNumber	curr_att;
4556			AttrNumber	nattrs = pg_class_tuple->relnatts;
4557
4558			for (curr_att = 1; curr_att <= nattrs; curr_att++)
4559			{
4560				HeapTuple	attTuple;
4561
4562				attTuple = SearchSysCache2(ATTNUM,
4563										   ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid),
4564										   Int16GetDatum(curr_att));
4565
4566				if (!HeapTupleIsValid(attTuple))
4567					continue;
4568
4569				/* when removing, remove all entries, even dropped columns */
4570
4571				recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, curr_att, NULL);
4572
4573				ReleaseSysCache(attTuple);
4574			}
4575		}
4576
4577		ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4578	}
4579
4580	/* Remove the record, if any, for the top-level object */
4581	recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, 0, NULL);
4582}
4583
4584/*
4585 * Record initial ACL for an extension object
4586 *
4587 * Can be called at any time, we check if 'creating_extension' is set and, if
4588 * not, exit immediately.
4589 *
4590 * Pass in the object OID, the OID of the class (the OID of the table which
4591 * the object is defined in) and the 'sub' id of the object (objsubid), if
4592 * any.  If there is no 'sub' id (they are currently only used for columns of
4593 * tables) then pass in '0'.  Finally, pass in the complete ACL to store.
4594 *
4595 * If an ACL already exists for this object/sub-object then we will replace
4596 * it with what is passed in.
4597 *
4598 * Passing in NULL for 'new_acl' will result in the entry for the object being
4599 * removed, if one is found.
4600 */
4601static void
4602recordExtensionInitPriv(Oid objoid, Oid classoid, int objsubid, Acl *new_acl)
4603{
4604	/*
4605	 * Generally, we only record the initial privileges when an extension is
4606	 * being created, but because we don't actually use CREATE EXTENSION
4607	 * during binary upgrades with pg_upgrade, there is a variable to let us
4608	 * know that the GRANT and REVOKE statements being issued, while this
4609	 * variable is true, are for the initial privileges of the extension
4610	 * object and therefore we need to record them.
4611	 */
4612	if (!creating_extension && !binary_upgrade_record_init_privs)
4613		return;
4614
4615	recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(objoid, classoid, objsubid, new_acl);
4616}
4617
4618/*
4619 * Record initial ACL for an extension object, worker.
4620 *
4621 * This will perform a wholesale replacement of the entire ACL for the object
4622 * passed in, therefore be sure to pass in the complete new ACL to use.
4623 *
4624 * Generally speaking, do *not* use this function directly but instead use
4625 * recordExtensionInitPriv(), which checks if 'creating_extension' is set.
4626 * This function does *not* check if 'creating_extension' is set as it is also
4627 * used when an object is added to or removed from an extension via ALTER
4628 * EXTENSION ... ADD/DROP.
4629 */
4630static void
4631recordExtensionInitPrivWorker(Oid objoid, Oid classoid, int objsubid,
4632							  Acl *new_acl)
4633{
4634	Relation	relation;
4635	ScanKeyData key[3];
4636	SysScanDesc scan;
4637	HeapTuple	tuple;
4638	HeapTuple	oldtuple;
4639	int			noldmembers;
4640	int			nnewmembers;
4641	Oid		   *oldmembers;
4642	Oid		   *newmembers;
4643
4644	/* We'll need the role membership of the new ACL. */
4645	nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
4646
4647	/* Search pg_init_privs for an existing entry. */
4648	relation = table_open(InitPrivsRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4649
4650	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
4651				Anum_pg_init_privs_objoid,
4652				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4653				ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid));
4654	ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
4655				Anum_pg_init_privs_classoid,
4656				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4657				ObjectIdGetDatum(classoid));
4658	ScanKeyInit(&key[2],
4659				Anum_pg_init_privs_objsubid,
4660				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_INT4EQ,
4661				Int32GetDatum(objsubid));
4662
4663	scan = systable_beginscan(relation, InitPrivsObjIndexId, true,
4664							  NULL, 3, key);
4665
4666	/* There should exist only one entry or none. */
4667	oldtuple = systable_getnext(scan);
4668
4669	/* If we find an entry, update it with the latest ACL. */
4670	if (HeapTupleIsValid(oldtuple))
4671	{
4672		Datum		values[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4673		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4674		bool		replace[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4675		Datum		oldAclDatum;
4676		bool		isNull;
4677		Acl		   *old_acl;
4678
4679		/* Update pg_shdepend for roles mentioned in the old/new ACLs. */
4680		oldAclDatum = heap_getattr(oldtuple, Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs,
4681								   RelationGetDescr(relation), &isNull);
4682		Assert(!isNull);
4683		old_acl = DatumGetAclP(oldAclDatum);
4684		noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
4685
4686		updateInitAclDependencies(classoid, objoid, objsubid,
4687								  noldmembers, oldmembers,
4688								  nnewmembers, newmembers);
4689
4690		/* If we have a new ACL to set, then update the row with it. */
4691		if (new_acl && ACL_NUM(new_acl) != 0)
4692		{
4693			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
4694			replace[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = true;
4695
4696			oldtuple = heap_modify_tuple(oldtuple, RelationGetDescr(relation),
4697										 values, nulls, replace);
4698
4699			CatalogTupleUpdate(relation, &oldtuple->t_self, oldtuple);
4700		}
4701		else
4702		{
4703			/* new_acl is NULL/empty, so delete the entry we found. */
4704			CatalogTupleDelete(relation, &oldtuple->t_self);
4705		}
4706	}
4707	else
4708	{
4709		Datum		values[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4710		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4711
4712		/*
4713		 * Only add a new entry if the new ACL is non-NULL.
4714		 *
4715		 * If we are passed in a NULL ACL and no entry exists, we can just
4716		 * fall through and do nothing.
4717		 */
4718		if (new_acl && ACL_NUM(new_acl) != 0)
4719		{
4720			/* No entry found, so add it. */
4721			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_objoid - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(objoid);
4722			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_classoid - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(classoid);
4723			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_objsubid - 1] = Int32GetDatum(objsubid);
4724
4725			/* This function only handles initial privileges of extensions */
4726			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_privtype - 1] =
4727				CharGetDatum(INITPRIVS_EXTENSION);
4728
4729			values[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
4730
4731			tuple = heap_form_tuple(RelationGetDescr(relation), values, nulls);
4732
4733			CatalogTupleInsert(relation, tuple);
4734
4735			/* Update pg_shdepend, too. */
4736			noldmembers = 0;
4737			oldmembers = NULL;
4738
4739			updateInitAclDependencies(classoid, objoid, objsubid,
4740									  noldmembers, oldmembers,
4741									  nnewmembers, newmembers);
4742		}
4743	}
4744
4745	systable_endscan(scan);
4746
4747	/* prevent error when processing objects multiple times */
4748	CommandCounterIncrement();
4749
4750	table_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
4751}
4752
4753/*
4754 * ReplaceRoleInInitPriv
4755 *
4756 * Used by shdepReassignOwned to replace mentions of a role in pg_init_privs.
4757 */
4758void
4759ReplaceRoleInInitPriv(Oid oldroleid, Oid newroleid,
4760					  Oid classid, Oid objid, int32 objsubid)
4761{
4762	Relation	rel;
4763	ScanKeyData key[3];
4764	SysScanDesc scan;
4765	HeapTuple	oldtuple;
4766	Datum		oldAclDatum;
4767	bool		isNull;
4768	Acl		   *old_acl;
4769	Acl		   *new_acl;
4770	HeapTuple	newtuple;
4771	int			noldmembers;
4772	int			nnewmembers;
4773	Oid		   *oldmembers;
4774	Oid		   *newmembers;
4775
4776	/* Search for existing pg_init_privs entry for the target object. */
4777	rel = table_open(InitPrivsRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4778
4779	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
4780				Anum_pg_init_privs_objoid,
4781				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4782				ObjectIdGetDatum(objid));
4783	ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
4784				Anum_pg_init_privs_classoid,
4785				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4786				ObjectIdGetDatum(classid));
4787	ScanKeyInit(&key[2],
4788				Anum_pg_init_privs_objsubid,
4789				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_INT4EQ,
4790				Int32GetDatum(objsubid));
4791
4792	scan = systable_beginscan(rel, InitPrivsObjIndexId, true,
4793							  NULL, 3, key);
4794
4795	/* There should exist only one entry or none. */
4796	oldtuple = systable_getnext(scan);
4797
4798	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(oldtuple))
4799	{
4800		/*
4801		 * Hmm, why are we here if there's no entry?  But pack up and go away
4802		 * quietly.
4803		 */
4804		systable_endscan(scan);
4805		table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4806		return;
4807	}
4808
4809	/* Get a writable copy of the existing ACL. */
4810	oldAclDatum = heap_getattr(oldtuple, Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs,
4811							   RelationGetDescr(rel), &isNull);
4812	Assert(!isNull);
4813	old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(oldAclDatum);
4814
4815	/*
4816	 * Generate new ACL.  This usage of aclnewowner is a bit off-label when
4817	 * oldroleid isn't the owner; but it does the job fine.
4818	 */
4819	new_acl = aclnewowner(old_acl, oldroleid, newroleid);
4820
4821	/*
4822	 * If we end with an empty ACL, delete the pg_init_privs entry.  (That
4823	 * probably can't happen here, but we may as well cover the case.)
4824	 */
4825	if (new_acl == NULL || ACL_NUM(new_acl) == 0)
4826	{
4827		CatalogTupleDelete(rel, &oldtuple->t_self);
4828	}
4829	else
4830	{
4831		Datum		values[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4832		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4833		bool		replaces[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4834
4835		/* Update existing entry. */
4836		values[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
4837		replaces[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = true;
4838
4839		newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(oldtuple, RelationGetDescr(rel),
4840									 values, nulls, replaces);
4841		CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
4842	}
4843
4844	/*
4845	 * Update the shared dependency ACL info.
4846	 */
4847	noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
4848	nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
4849
4850	updateInitAclDependencies(classid, objid, objsubid,
4851							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
4852							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
4853
4854	systable_endscan(scan);
4855
4856	/* prevent error when processing objects multiple times */
4857	CommandCounterIncrement();
4858
4859	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4860}
4861
4862/*
4863 * RemoveRoleFromInitPriv
4864 *
4865 * Used by shdepDropOwned to remove mentions of a role in pg_init_privs.
4866 */
4867void
4868RemoveRoleFromInitPriv(Oid roleid, Oid classid, Oid objid, int32 objsubid)
4869{
4870	Relation	rel;
4871	ScanKeyData key[3];
4872	SysScanDesc scan;
4873	HeapTuple	oldtuple;
4874	int			cacheid;
4875	HeapTuple	objtuple;
4876	Oid			ownerId;
4877	Datum		oldAclDatum;
4878	bool		isNull;
4879	Acl		   *old_acl;
4880	Acl		   *new_acl;
4881	HeapTuple	newtuple;
4882	int			noldmembers;
4883	int			nnewmembers;
4884	Oid		   *oldmembers;
4885	Oid		   *newmembers;
4886
4887	/* Search for existing pg_init_privs entry for the target object. */
4888	rel = table_open(InitPrivsRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4889
4890	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
4891				Anum_pg_init_privs_objoid,
4892				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4893				ObjectIdGetDatum(objid));
4894	ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
4895				Anum_pg_init_privs_classoid,
4896				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4897				ObjectIdGetDatum(classid));
4898	ScanKeyInit(&key[2],
4899				Anum_pg_init_privs_objsubid,
4900				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_INT4EQ,
4901				Int32GetDatum(objsubid));
4902
4903	scan = systable_beginscan(rel, InitPrivsObjIndexId, true,
4904							  NULL, 3, key);
4905
4906	/* There should exist only one entry or none. */
4907	oldtuple = systable_getnext(scan);
4908
4909	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(oldtuple))
4910	{
4911		/*
4912		 * Hmm, why are we here if there's no entry?  But pack up and go away
4913		 * quietly.
4914		 */
4915		systable_endscan(scan);
4916		table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4917		return;
4918	}
4919
4920	/* Get a writable copy of the existing ACL. */
4921	oldAclDatum = heap_getattr(oldtuple, Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs,
4922							   RelationGetDescr(rel), &isNull);
4923	Assert(!isNull);
4924	old_acl = DatumGetAclPCopy(oldAclDatum);
4925
4926	/*
4927	 * We need the members of both old and new ACLs so we can correct the
4928	 * shared dependency information.  Collect data before
4929	 * merge_acl_with_grant throws away old_acl.
4930	 */
4931	noldmembers = aclmembers(old_acl, &oldmembers);
4932
4933	/* Must find out the owner's OID the hard way. */
4934	cacheid = get_object_catcache_oid(classid);
4935	objtuple = SearchSysCache1(cacheid, ObjectIdGetDatum(objid));
4936	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(objtuple))
4937		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for %s %u",
4938			 get_object_class_descr(classid), objid);
4939
4940	ownerId = DatumGetObjectId(SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(cacheid,
4941													  objtuple,
4942													  get_object_attnum_owner(classid)));
4943	ReleaseSysCache(objtuple);
4944
4945	/*
4946	 * Generate new ACL.  Grantor of rights is always the same as the owner.
4947	 */
4948	if (old_acl != NULL)
4949		new_acl = merge_acl_with_grant(old_acl,
4950									   false,	/* is_grant */
4951									   false,	/* grant_option */
4952									   DROP_RESTRICT,
4953									   list_make1_oid(roleid),
4954									   ACLITEM_ALL_PRIV_BITS,
4955									   ownerId,
4956									   ownerId);
4957	else
4958		new_acl = NULL;			/* this case shouldn't happen, probably */
4959
4960	/* If we end with an empty ACL, delete the pg_init_privs entry. */
4961	if (new_acl == NULL || ACL_NUM(new_acl) == 0)
4962	{
4963		CatalogTupleDelete(rel, &oldtuple->t_self);
4964	}
4965	else
4966	{
4967		Datum		values[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4968		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4969		bool		replaces[Natts_pg_init_privs] = {0};
4970
4971		/* Update existing entry. */
4972		values[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = PointerGetDatum(new_acl);
4973		replaces[Anum_pg_init_privs_initprivs - 1] = true;
4974
4975		newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(oldtuple, RelationGetDescr(rel),
4976									 values, nulls, replaces);
4977		CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
4978	}
4979
4980	/*
4981	 * Update the shared dependency ACL info.
4982	 */
4983	nnewmembers = aclmembers(new_acl, &newmembers);
4984
4985	updateInitAclDependencies(classid, objid, objsubid,
4986							  noldmembers, oldmembers,
4987							  nnewmembers, newmembers);
4988
4989	systable_endscan(scan);
4990
4991	/* prevent error when processing objects multiple times */
4992	CommandCounterIncrement();
4993
4994	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4995}
